elements.text: ❑ Describe repair or reconstruction that is generally consistent with the damage or
elements.label: text

elements.text: destruction suffered by the residence, as reported in the proof of loss filed by the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower with the property insurer and as documented by the insurer’s adjuster
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Establish a reasonable schedule for completion of each phase of repair or
elements.label: text

elements.text: reconstruction
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Borrower and the contractor have executed a contract by which they agree to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The contractor will perform the work described in the plans and specifications
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The contractor will comply with applicable codes and regulations governing
elements.label: text

elements.text: residential repair or reconstruction (including, but not limited to, building codes and
elements.label: text

elements.text: zoning, permit and inspection regulations).
elements.label: text

elements.text: These codes and regulations may vary from State to State and, within the same State,
elements.label: text

elements.text: from county to county. Therefore, Servicers should have adequate measures in place
elements.label: text

elements.text: to verify contractors’ and inspectors’ compliance certifications to protect Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac’s and the Servicer’s respective investments.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If additional funds are needed to bring a damaged or destroyed residence into
elements.label: text

elements.text: compliance with applicable codes and regulations, the Borrowers should determine
elements.label: text

elements.text: whether their property insurer will waive any policy provision restricting payment for
elements.label: text

elements.text: the increased cost of construction resulting from enforcement of codes and
elements.label: text

elements.text: regulations.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ A specified dollar amount is the maximum amount that the contractor may charge for
elements.label: text

elements.text: the work
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The contractor will be paid a specified advance (if applicable, usually not exceeding
elements.label: text

elements.text: 10% of the total contract amount) and, subsequently, on a specified draw schedule
elements.label: text

elements.text: contingent on verification of satisfactory completion of specified work phases. If the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage status at time of notification is 31 or more days delinquent at time of loss,
elements.label: text

elements.text: released funds must not exceed 25% increments of insurance loss proceeds.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The contractor, its subcontractors and its material suppliers will provide written
elements.label: text

elements.text: acknowledgment of payment for work performed and materials supplied and the
elements.label: text

elements.text: necessary lien waivers or releases so that the Mortgaged Premises may remain clear
elements.label: text

elements.text: of all such liens and encumbrances
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Each scheduled work phase has been satisfactorily completed in accordance with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: plans and specifications in the contract
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer may choose to have the above-described oversight functions performed by
elements.label: text

elements.text: its staff or by a third party (such as a specialized firm or another Servicer). However, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer is liable for the performance of any third party it retains. The third party may be
elements.label: text

elements.text: compensated from insurance proceeds retained by the Servicer only to the extent agreed
elements.label: text

elements.text: to by the Borrower and allowed under applicable law.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e) Final inspection of the Mortgaged Premises
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must follow the requirements below regarding the final inspection.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Requirements for final inspection
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage current or less than 31
elements.label: text

elements.text: days delinquent at the time of
elements.label: text

elements.text: loss
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage 31 or more days
elements.label: text

elements.text: delinquent at the time of loss
elements.label: text

elements.text: A final inspection is not required. A final inspection is always
elements.label: text

elements.text: required to ensure all repairs are
elements.label: text

elements.text: completed.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 8202.6: Lender-Placed Insurance (LPI) (07/09/25)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains information related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Insurance coverage requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Defined term for Lender-Place Insurance (LPI)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Required deductible for LPI covered dwelling
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Insurance coverage requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller/Servicer must require the Borrower to obtain appropriate insurance coverage in
elements.label: text

elements.text: accordance with the terms of the Security Instrument, the Guide and applicable law.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must continuously monitor the Borrower and Mortgage to ensure that adequate
elements.label: text

elements.text: coverage has been obtained and remains in force. If the Borrower’s coverage is canceled or
elements.label: text

elements.text: in jeopardy of cancelation due to non-payment of premium, the Servicer must attempt to
elements.label: text

elements.text: continue coverage by paying the premium on behalf of the Borrower in accordance with
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable law. If the Borrower and the Servicer do not or cannot continue such coverage or
elements.label: text

elements.text: if the coverage obtained is canceled or lapses, the Servicer must obtain LPI.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must adjust the Borrower’s Escrow payments accordingly or bill the Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: to recover the advance if the Servicer does not maintain an Escrow account for the Borrower.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Borrower fails to reimburse the Servicer, the Servicer may recommend acceleration to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac for the Borrower’s default under the terms of the Security Instrument.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the additional coverage cannot be obtained, the Servicer must immediately make
elements.label: text

elements.text: appropriate recommendations to Freddie Mac (see Directory 5).
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer obtains LPI coverage for the Borrower from an LPI carrier in accordance with
elements.label: text

elements.text: this section, the following apply:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Servicer or agents, brokers or other entities affiliated with the Servicer may not
elements.label: text

elements.text: receive any compensation in the form of commissions or similar incentive-based
elements.label: text

elements.text: compensation regardless of its designation as commission, bonus, fees or other type of
elements.label: text

elements.text: payment from LPI carriers; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Servicer may not use its own affiliated entities to insure or reinsure LPI
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Defined term for LPI
elements.label: text

elements.text: For purposes of this section an “entity” is “affiliated” with the Servicer when it is owned or
elements.label: text

elements.text: controlled, in whole or in part, by the Servicer, including, but not limited to, a subsidiary of
elements.label: text

elements.text: or in a joint venture or partnership with the Servicer. An affiliated entity shall also include
elements.label: text

elements.text: any entity that owns or controls, in whole or in part, the Servicer (e.g., the parent company of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Servicer) and any entity that is under common ownership or control with the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e.g., two subsidiaries of the same parent company). An affiliated entity shall not include a
elements.label: text

elements.text: publicly traded company of whose stock the Servicer owns less than 5%.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Required deductible for LPI policies
elements.label: text

elements.text: The required deductible is based on the dwelling coverage amount provided by the LPI
elements.label: text

elements.text: policy. These deductibles are required for all perils except wind, hail and flood, to the extent
elements.label: text

elements.text: permissible under applicable State law. Required deductibles for LPI policies are provided in
elements.label: text

elements.text: the table below:
elements.label: text

elements.text: LPI policy coverage requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: Dwelling coverage Deductible
elements.label: text

elements.text: $100,000 up to and including $250,000 $2,000
elements.label: text

elements.text: Greater than $250,000 $2,500
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must comply with all applicable law pertaining to administration of LPI,
elements.label: text

elements.text: including providing notices to the Borrower and refund of premium for duplicate coverage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must, upon request from Freddie Mac, provide a copy of the insurance policy
elements.label: text

elements.text: and any and all agreements with any LPI carrier used by the Servicer on Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: demonstrating compliance with the above requirements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Servicer’s failure to comply with the above requirements may result in any of the remedies
elements.label: text

elements.text: available to Freddie Mac in Section 3601.1.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 8203: Mortgage Insurance
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: 8203.1: Mortgage insurance terms, warranties and
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: other requirements (07/09/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains information related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Mortgage insurance defined terms
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ General warranties and covenants and other requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Pre-HPA Mortgage that is modified
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Determination of property securing the Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Other conditions for Borrower-requested cancelation of Borrower-paid mortgage insurance
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Borrowers impacted by an Eligible Disaster
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Mortgage insurance defined terms
elements.label: text

elements.text: The following terms apply for purposes of this chapter:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Defined terms related to the mortgage insurance
elements.label: text

elements.text: H
elements.label: text

elements.text: HPA The Homeowners Protection Act of 1998, as amended
elements.label: text

elements.text: HPA Effective Date July 29, 1999, the first date on which requirements of the HPA
elements.label: text

elements.text: applied to any HPA Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: HPA Mortgage A 1-unit Primary Residence Mortgage with mortgage insurance
elements.label: text

elements.text: originated on or after the HPA Effective Date. For purposes of
elements.label: text

elements.text: canceling mortgage insurance in accordance with the requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: of Sections 8203.2 and 8203.4(a), an “HPA Mortgage” also includes
elements.label: text

elements.text: any second home Mortgage (as defined in Section 4201.12) with
elements.label: text

elements.text: mortgage insurance and any Pre-HPA Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: HPA Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: Annual Notice
elements.label: text

elements.text: A written statement that, pursuant to the HPA, the Seller/Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: must send annually to a Borrower concerning the Borrower’s HPA
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage. The statement informs the Borrower of mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: insurance cancelation and termination rights pertaining to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower’s HPA Mortgage. The HPA requires different notices, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: contents of which depend on whether the HPA Mortgage is a fixed￾rate or ARM.
elements.label: text

elements.text: N
elements.label: text

elements.text: Non-HPA Mortgage Any Home Mortgage with mortgage insurance, originated on or
elements.label: text

elements.text: after the HPA Effective Date, that is not an HPA Mortgage. For
elements.label: text

elements.text: purposes of canceling mortgage insurance in accordance with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements of Section 8203.3, a “Non-HPA Mortgage” also
elements.label: text

elements.text: includes any Pre-HPA Mortgage secured by a 2- to 4-
elements.label: text

elements.text: unit Mortgaged Premises or 1- to 4-unit Investment Property.
elements.label: text

elements.text: P
elements.label: text

elements.text: Pre-HPA Mortgage Any Home Mortgage with mortgage insurance originated before the
elements.label: text

elements.text: HPA Effective Date
elements.label: text

elements.text: Pre-HPA Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: Annual Notice
elements.label: text

elements.text: A written statement that, pursuant to the HPA, the Seller/Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: must send annually to a Borrower concerning the Borrower’s Pre￾HPA Mortgage. The statement gives instructions to the Borrower on
elements.label: text

elements.text: how to contact the Seller/Servicer to determine if and how mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: insurance may be canceled.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) General warranties and covenants and other requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller/Servicer warrants that mortgage insurance has been obtained as required in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 4701.1 and agrees and covenants that such mortgage insurance will be maintained
elements.label: text

elements.text: unless canceled in accordance with the requirements of Section 4701.1 (for purposes of
elements.label: text

elements.text: determining the “value” of Mortgaged Premises located in the State of New York), Sections
elements.label: text

elements.text: 8203.2 through 8203.4(c) or pursuant to applicable law. Additionally, some of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements in Sections 8203.2 and 8203.4 are derived from, and are in addition to,
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements under the HPA.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller/Servicer also warrants that the Borrower has been given, and agrees and covenants
elements.label: text

elements.text: to provide, all disclosures required by applicable law, including, but not limited to, the HPA,
elements.label: text

elements.text: relating to the terms on which the Borrower-paid mortgage insurance may be canceled. Such
elements.label: text

elements.text: disclosures may include an initial HPA-related notice given to a Borrower at the Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: closing and an HPA Mortgage Annual Notice or Pre-HPA Mortgage Annual Notice, as
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Pre-HPA Mortgage that is modified
elements.label: text

elements.text: The modification of a Pre-HPA Mortgage (a fixed-rate, adjustable-rate) does not cause the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Pre-HPA Mortgage to become an HPA Mortgage or trigger any new obligation under this
elements.label: text

elements.text: chapter relating to the Pre-HPA Mortgage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Determination of property securing the Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: For mortgage insurance cancelation purposes, the occupancy of the Mortgaged Premises as
elements.label: text

elements.text: stated in the Borrower’s Mortgage application must be used to determine whether the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgaged Premises is a Primary Residence, a second home (as defined in Section 4201.12)
elements.label: text

elements.text: or an Investment Property.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e) Other conditions for Borrower-requested cancelation of Borrower-paid mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: insurance
elements.label: text

elements.text: When a Borrower requests cancelation of Borrower-paid mortgage insurance pursuant to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Sections 8203.2 and 8203.3, except as may otherwise be provided in a Purchase Document,
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Seller/Servicer must not request or require that a Borrower make a written certification or
elements.label: text

elements.text: any other representation concerning the existence or nonexistence of a Second Mortgage or
elements.label: text

elements.text: other subordinate lien on the Mortgaged Premises. If a Purchase Document contains a
elements.label: text

elements.text: condition relating to such a written certification or representation by a Borrower, the Seller
elements.label: text

elements.text: and the Servicer of the Borrower’s Mortgage must provide to the Borrower all disclosures
elements.label: text

elements.text: required by applicable law with respect to the condition set forth in the Purchase Document.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (f) Borrowers impacted by an Eligible Disaster
elements.label: text

elements.text: When a Borrower impacted by an Eligible Disaster that resulted in the Mortgage being
elements.label: text

elements.text: subject to a disaster-related forbearance plan pursuant to Chapter 8404 or other Purchase
elements.label: text

elements.text: Documents requests to cancel Borrower-paid mortgage insurance post-forbearance and after
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Mortgage has been restored to a current status, any Delinquency that was a direct result of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Mortgage being subject to a disaster-related forbearance plan, and, following the disaster￾related forbearance, transition to a relief or workout option to cure the Delinquency (e.g.,
elements.label: text

elements.text: repayment plan or Trial Period Plan) in accordance with Section 8404.1(f) or other Purchase
elements.label: text

elements.text: Documents, must be considered an exception to the payment history requirements set forth in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Sections 8203.2 and 8203.3.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 8203.2: Borrower-requested cancelation of Borrower￾paid mortgage insurance on an HPA Mortgage
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Borrower-requested cancelation of Borrower-paid mortgage insurance based on the original
elements.label: text

elements.text: value
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Borrower-requested cancelation of Borrower-paid mortgage insurance based on the current
elements.label: text

elements.text: value
elements.label: text

elements.text: For Borrower-requested cancelation of Borrower-paid mortgage insurance, unless otherwise
elements.label: text

elements.text: canceled pursuant to applicable law, the Seller/Servicer must cancel such mortgage insurance
elements.label: text

elements.text: when the Borrower and the HPA Mortgage meet the requirements of Section 8203.2(a) or
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 8203.2(b), respectively.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In addition to processing and/or responding to Borrowers’ written or verbal requests to cancel
elements.label: text

elements.text: mortgage insurance in accordance with this section, the Seller/Servicer also may:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Systematically identify all Mortgages that the Seller/Servicer services for Freddie Mac that
elements.label: text

elements.text: may be close to, or have reached, the applicable mortgage insurance cancelation point set
elements.label: text

elements.text: forth below; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Notify the Borrowers of such Mortgages of next steps, if any, that must be taken to determine
elements.label: text

elements.text: if mortgage insurance may be canceled
elements.label: text

elements.text: Upon obtaining an eligible Borrower’s verbal and/or written request or affirmation, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer must cancel the related mortgage insurance upon ascertaining that all applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: conditions have been met.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Borrower-requested cancelation of Borrower-paid mortgage insurance based on the
elements.label: text

elements.text: original value
elements.label: text

elements.text: The table below lists the requirements for Borrower-requested cancelation of Borrower-paid
elements.label: text

elements.text: mortgage insurance based on the original value (as “value” is defined in Section 4203.1):
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower-requested cancelation of Borrower-paid mortgage insurance based on the
elements.label: text

elements.text: original value*
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cancelation point
elements.label: text

elements.text: Based on the original value, the loan-to-value (LTV) ratio must be 80% or less. (Note: As
elements.label: text

elements.text: opposed to the conditions set forth in row 1, Cancelation point, of Section 8203.2(b), there
elements.label: text

elements.text: is no required minimum period having elapsed since the Origination Date of a Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: when determining the cancelation point.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: At the option of the Borrower, the numerator of the LTV ratio may be based on:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The amortization schedule (irrespective of the Mortgage’s current UPB); or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Mortgage’s current UPB (based on actual payments collected)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Regarding the amortization schedule in the cancelation point requirements set forth
elements.label: text

elements.text: above, this amortization schedule is the initial amortization schedule for a fixed-rate
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage or current amortization schedule following the most recent rate change for an
elements.label: text

elements.text: ARM or Step-Rate Mortgage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Evidence of value
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller/Servicer must warrant that the original value of the Mortgaged Premises, at a
elements.label: text

elements.text: minimum, supports the LTV ratio required to cancel mortgage insurance.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Payment history
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Borrower’s payment history must show:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Mortgage is current
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ There was no payment 30 days or more past due in the preceding 12 months (or since
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Origination Date if the Mortgage was originated in the past 12 months); and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ There was no payment 60 days or more past due in the preceding 24 months (or since
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Origination Date if the Mortgage was originated in the past 24 months)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Pursuant to Section 8203.1(f) regarding Borrowers impacted by an Eligible Disaster, any
elements.label: text

elements.text: Delinquency that is a direct result of the Mortgage being subject to a disaster-related
elements.label: text

elements.text: forbearance plan and, following the disaster-related forbearance, transition to a relief or
elements.label: text

elements.text: workout option to cure the Delinquency (e.g., repayment plan or Trial Period Plan) in
elements.label: text

elements.text: accordance with Section 8404.1(f) or other Purchase Documents, must be considered an
elements.label: text

elements.text: exception to the payment history requirements set forth above.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: The reference to a “preceding” period in the payment history requirements set forth
elements.label: text

elements.text: above means the specified time period that immediately preceded the later of: (i) the date
elements.label: text

elements.text: on which the required LTV ratio was reached or (ii) the date on which the Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: submits the request to cancel mortgage insurance.
elements.label: text

elements.text: *”Value” is defined in Section 4203.1.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Borrower-requested cancelation of Borrower-paid mortgage insurance based on the
elements.label: text

elements.text: current value
elements.label: text

elements.text: The table below lists the requirements for Borrower-requested cancelation of Borrower-paid
elements.label: text

elements.text: mortgage insurance based on the current value:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower-requested cancelation of Borrower-paid mortgage insurance based on the
elements.label: text

elements.text: current value*
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cancelation point
elements.label: text

elements.text: Based on the Mortgage’s current UPB and the current value, the LTV ratio must be:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ 75% or less if the Mortgage is seasoned between 2 and 5 years
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ 80% or less if the Mortgage is seasoned greater than 5 years
elements.label: text

elements.text: The minimum two-year seasoning requirement is waived if the Servicer determines the
elements.label: text

elements.text: increased market value of the Mortgaged Premises since the Origination Date of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage is due to substantial improvements; the LTV ratio must be 80% or less.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Regarding canceling mortgage insurance because of substantial improvements to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgaged Premises, the following conditions must be met:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The market value of the Mortgaged Premises must be calculated using the current
elements.label: text

elements.text: market value estimate in a BPO or an appraisal that is ordered and obtained in
elements.label: text

elements.text: accordance with row 2 of this table, Evidence of value, and prepared after the
elements.label: text

elements.text: substantial improvements have been completed
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The substantial improvements must conform to local zoning and building codes; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The BPO or appraisal must state the specific nature, extent and cost of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: improvements made and the effect of the improvements on the current estimated
elements.label: text

elements.text: market value
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: The reference to “substantial improvements” in the cancelation point requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: set forth above means that the improvements, made since the Origination Date, were any
elements.label: text

elements.text: type of renovation(s) that substantially extended the useful life of the Mortgaged
elements.label: text

elements.text: Premises.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Examples of “substantial improvements” include, but are not limited to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Significant structural alterations (including addition of square footage)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Construction requiring permits
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Adding new components/appliances that previously didn’t exist (e.g., adding a
elements.label: text

elements.text: bathtub, hardwood flooring, central air conditioning)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Replacing components/appliances with energy-efficient versions
elements.label: text

elements.text: Repairs made to keep the Mortgaged Premises maintained and fully functional (e.g.,
elements.label: text

elements.text: replacing roofing/flooring/appliances with same materials) are not considered
elements.label: text

elements.text: “substantial improvements.”
elements.label: text

elements.text: Evidence of value
elements.label: text

elements.text: At the Borrower’s expense, and performed no later than 120 days after the date on which
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Borrower submits a request to cancel mortgage insurance, the Seller/Servicer must
elements.label: text

elements.text: verify the current value by one of the following methods:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A new BPO ordered and obtained through BPOdirect®, unless applicable law
elements.label: text

elements.text: requires that an appraisal be used or the Seller/Servicer determines that an appraisal is
elements.label: text

elements.text: in the Borrower’s best interest (e.g., at the option of the Borrower). The BPO must be
elements.label: text

elements.text: an interior and exterior BPO. (Refer to Sections 2406.1 and 9202.4(b) for details
elements.label: text

elements.text: regarding Freddie Mac’s process to obtain a property value.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If applicable law requires that an appraisal be used or the Seller/Servicer determines
elements.label: text

elements.text: that an appraisal is in the Borrower’s best interest, a new appraisal with an interior
elements.label: text

elements.text: and exterior inspection. The Seller/Servicer may order and obtain the appraisal either
elements.label: text

elements.text: directly from Freddie Mac through BPOdirect or from an appraiser of its choice. If a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer does not obtain the appraisal directly from Freddie Mac, then the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer must ensure that the appraisal complies with the requirements of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Topic 5600.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Prior to ordering a BPO or an appraisal in conjunction with a request to cancel

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Automatic cancelation of Borrower-paid mortgage insurance on an HPA Mortgage

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Automatic cancelation of Borrower-paid mortgage insurance on an HPA Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: For automatic cancelation of Borrower-paid mortgage insurance, unless otherwise canceled
elements.label: text

elements.text: pursuant to applicable law, the Seller/Servicer must cancel such mortgage insurance when
elements.label: text

elements.text: the HPA Mortgage meets the requirements of this section.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Non-HPA Mortgages are not eligible for automatic cancelation of Borrower-paid
elements.label: text

elements.text: mortgage insurance. (Refer to Section 8203.3 regarding Borrower-requested cancelation of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower-paid mortgage insurance for Non-HPA Mortgages and Seller/Servicer solicitations
elements.label: text

elements.text: of such requests.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: The table below lists the requirements for automatic cancelation of Borrower-paid mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: insurance:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Requirements for automatic cancelation of Borrower-paid mortgage insurance
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cancelation point
elements.label: text

elements.text: Provided that the payment record requirements set forth in row 2 of this table, Payment
elements.label: text

elements.text: record, are met, the earlier of:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The date on which the loan-to-value (LTV) ratio is first scheduled to reach 78% based
elements.label: text

elements.text: on the original value (as “value” is defined in Section 4203.1) and the amortization
elements.label: text

elements.text: schedule (irrespective of the Mortgage’s current UPB); or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The date on which the midpoint of the amortization period of the Mortgage is reached.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The midpoint occurs halfway through a Mortgage’s amortization period based upon
elements.label: text

elements.text: the amortization schedule.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Example: In the case of a 360-month or 30-year Mortgage with a payment Due Date
elements.label: text

elements.text: on the first of each month, the midpoint is deemed to be the first day of the 180th
elements.label: text

elements.text: month. Assuming that the payment record requirements set forth in row 2 of this table,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Payment record, are met, the Seller/Servicer must have mortgage insurance canceled
elements.label: text

elements.text: effective for the 181st month’s payment.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Regarding the amortization schedule in the cancelation point requirements set forth
elements.label: text

elements.text: above, this amortization schedule is the initial amortization schedule for a fixed-rate
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage or current amortization schedule following the most recent rate change for an
elements.label: text

elements.text: ARM or Step-Rate Mortgage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Payment record
elements.label: text

elements.text: All principal and interest and Escrow installments (or all Seller/Servicer advances for
elements.label: text

elements.text: unpaid charges otherwise payable from Escrow, if applicable) with Due Dates prior to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: cancelation point must be paid by the Borrower on or before the cancelation point. (In the
elements.label: text

elements.text: example set forth in row 1 of this table, Cancelation point, all such installments with Due
elements.label: text

elements.text: Dates before the 180th month must have been paid by the Borrower by the first day of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: 180th month in order for mortgage insurance to be canceled effective for the 181st month’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: payment.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: If any installment of principal and interest and Escrow (or any Seller/Servicer advance for
elements.label: text

elements.text: unpaid charges otherwise payable from Escrow, if applicable) with a Due Date prior to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: cancelation point has not been paid by the Borrower on or before the cancelation point,
elements.label: text

elements.text: then the cancelation point must be deferred to the first day of the first month beginning
elements.label: text

elements.text: after the date on which all such installments are paid by the Borrower.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Cancelation of Borrower-paid mortgage insurance on modified Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: For the purpose of canceling mortgage insurance in accordance with the requirements of this
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 8203 on a modified Mortgage (HPA Mortgage, Non-HPA Mortgage or Pre-HPA
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage), the required LTV ratio and the amortization schedule or period, as applicable,

elements.text: must be based upon the modified terms of the Mortgage. (See Section 9206.1(a) for examples

elements.text: (c) Cancelation of Borrower-paid mortgage insurance on refinance Mortgages

elements.text: For the purpose of canceling mortgage insurance in accordance with the requirements of this

elements.text: Chapter 8203 on a refinance Mortgage (HPA Mortgage, Non-HPA Mortgage or Pre-HPA

elements.text: Mortgage), the original value is the appraised value relied upon to approve the refinance
elements.label: text

elements.text: transaction.

elements.text: (d) Cancelation of lender-paid mortgage insurance
elements.label: text

elements.text: Lender-paid mortgage insurance is not cancelable. The Seller/Servicer must provide to the

elements.text: Borrower all related disclosures required by the HPA, if applicable.
