elements.text: 8203.5: FHA insurance, mortgage insurance transfers
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: and cancelations (07/09/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ FHA insurance
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Transfers of mortgage insurance coverage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Reporting mortgage insurance cancelations
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) FHA insurance
elements.label: text

elements.text: FHA insurance must not be canceled while Freddie Mac has an ownership interest in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage. The Seller/Servicer must, however, discontinue the collection of FHA insurance
elements.label: text

elements.text: premiums from a Borrower automatically or pursuant to a Borrower’s request in accordance
elements.label: text

elements.text: with applicable law and FHA regulations.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Transfers of mortgage insurance coverage
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller/Servicer must not transfer the mortgage insurance coverage in effect at the time
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Seller/Servicer delivered a Mortgage to Freddie Mac, unless:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Coverage must be replaced in conjunction with a third party’s assumption of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower’s Mortgage obligations
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The existing MI does not renew the existing coverage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Freddie Mac has terminated the approval status of the existing MI; or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Freddie Mac has instructed the Seller/Servicer to transfer the coverage
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Reporting mortgage insurance cancelations
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Seller/Servicer reporting requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller/Servicer must report the following mortgage insurance cancelation data to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Cancelation date
elements.label: text

elements.text: The cancelation date is defined as the date the Seller/Servicer specifies to the MI that
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Seller/Servicer canceled the mortgage insurance.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Cancelation reason code
elements.label: text

elements.text: The cancelation reason code is a two-place, numeric-alpha code as follows:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ 1M — Cancelation resulting from a Borrower request based on original value of
elements.label: text

elements.text: property in accordance with Sections 8203.2 through 8203.3 or applicable law
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ 1N — Cancelation resulting from a Borrower request based on current value of
elements.label: text

elements.text: property in accordance with Sections 8203.2 through 8203.3 or applicable law
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ 1O — Cancelation based on automatic cancelation provisions of Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 8203.4(a) or applicable law
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Seller/Servicer reporting timeline
elements.label: text

elements.text: If there is no exception activity to report for a Mortgage whose mortgage insurance the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer has canceled, the Seller/Servicer must report the mortgage insurance
elements.label: text

elements.text: cancelation data with its monthly loan-level accounting data within three Accounting
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cycles after the calendar month of the cancelation date. For example, if the cancelation
elements.label: text

elements.text: date is April 5th, the Seller/Servicer must report the mortgage insurance cancelation data
elements.label: text

elements.text: with its monthly loan-level accounting data no later than the July Accounting Cycle.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If there is exception activity to report for the Mortgage, the Seller/Servicer must defer
elements.label: text

elements.text: reporting the mortgage insurance cancelation data until the first available Accounting
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cycle in which there is no exception activity to report for that Mortgage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Refer to Section 8301.2(c) for requirements regarding Freddie Mac’s reporting
elements.label: text

elements.text: media.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 8203.6: Directing MI disclosure of Mortgage
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: information to Freddie Mac and reconciliation
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Disclosure of MI Mortgage information
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Reconciliation of MI Mortgage information
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Disclosure of MI Mortgage information
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller/Servicer must instruct, in writing, any MI providing current or prospective
elements.label: text

elements.text: coverage for Freddie Mac Mortgages in accordance with Section 4701.1 to disclose to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac (upon Freddie Mac’s request) any and all information, data and materials
elements.label: text

elements.text: pertaining to any Freddie Mac Mortgage sold to and/or serviced for Freddie Mac by the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller/Servicer must continue to meet its obligations under the Purchase Documents to
elements.label: text

elements.text: provide Freddie Mac information regarding Freddie Mac Mortgages regardless of whether
elements.label: text

elements.text: the MI has provided, or may provide, required information and/or materials to Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Reconciliation of MI Mortgage information
elements.label: text

elements.text: Periodically, Freddie Mac will reconcile mortgage insurance information provided by an MI
elements.label: text

elements.text: with the mortgage insurance ULDD Data Points (see Section 6302.21) provided when loans
elements.label: text

elements.text: are delivered to Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If discrepancies are identified, Freddie Mac will post data files containing the selected loans
elements.label: text

elements.text: and mortgage insurance ULDD Data Points to the Total MI Reconciliation tool. (See
elements.label: text

elements.text: Exhibit 88, Servicing Tools.) Within 10 Business Days of notification, Servicers must access
elements.label: text

elements.text: the data files and provide updated information through the Total MI Reconciliation user
elements.label: text

elements.text: interface in Freddie Mac Gateway or the Total MI Reconciliation application programming
elements.label: text

elements.text: interface. If the Servicer does not provide updated information within 10 Business Days, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: selected loans may be referred to Servicing Remedy Management for review.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Servicers should refer to Exhibit 10, Freddie Mac-Approved Mortgage Insurers, for the
elements.label: text

elements.text: number of digits in each MI’s certificate number.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 8301: Basics of Investor Accounting
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: 8301.1: Servicer’s responsibilities for MBS,
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: participation certificates, investor accounting and
elements.label: text

elements.text: reporting performance standards (08/13/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Servicer’s responsibilities in connection with MBS and participation certificates
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Investor accounting responsibilities
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Investor reporting performance standards
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Servicer’s responsibilities in connection with MBS and participation certificates
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer’s responsibilities in connection with MBS and participation certificates (i.e.,
elements.label: text

elements.text: UMBS®, MBS and WAC ARM PCs) backed by Freddie Mac-owned Mortgages include,
elements.label: text

elements.text: among other things, collecting and accounting for payments received from Borrowers and
elements.label: text

elements.text: passing through principal and interest to Freddie Mac from which Freddie Mac passes
elements.label: text

elements.text: through the amount of principal and/or interest due to the UMBS, MBS or WAC ARM PC
elements.label: text

elements.text: holders.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The accuracy and timeliness of the Servicer’s performance of these responsibilities are vital
elements.label: text

elements.text: and are part of the Servicer’s warranties to Freddie Mac and Freddie Mac’s UMBS, MBS and
elements.label: text

elements.text: WAC ARM PC holders.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Investor accounting responsibilities
elements.label: text

elements.text: Once Freddie Mac purchases a Mortgage from a Seller that also services the Mortgage or a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Transferee Servicer that assumes the Freddie Mac Servicing Contract and related Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Contract Rights (as defined in Section 1101.2(c)), the Servicer must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Maintain accurate Servicing records
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Deposit all required funds into the appropriate Custodial Accounts
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Report to Freddie Mac throughout the month and monthly as required
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Resolve any discrepancies between the Servicer’s and Freddie Mac’s records
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must comply with the requirements in this chapter and Chapters 8302, 8303 and
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Cash accounting
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer’s responsibilities in administering funds it receives for Freddie Mac-owned
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages include:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Collecting, processing and depositing Borrowers’ payments to the correct Custodial
elements.label: text

elements.text: Accounts
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Administering the Escrow accounts
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Investor reporting and drafting of funds
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer’s responsibilities for reporting to Freddie Mac include:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Reporting all loan activity and events to Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Reporting current information for each Mortgage that accurately reflects the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower’s loan activity and loan history
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Ensuring accounts are adequately funded to allow Freddie Mac to draft principal,
elements.label: text

elements.text: interest and compensatory fees due to Freddie Mac by no later than the applicable due
elements.label: text

elements.text: dates
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Reconciling the Servicer’s cash accounting and investor reporting systems with
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac’s loan-level accounting records
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii) Custodial Accounts management
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer’s responsibilities for managing the Custodial Accounts for Freddie Mac’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: funds include ensuring that:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Custodial Accounts are safeguarded, properly segregated and maintained at an
elements.label: text

elements.text: Eligible Depository and that Freddie Mac is authorized to draft funds from the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Custodial Accounts
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Custodial Accounts are fully funded and reconciled
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Form 59, Principal and Interest Custodial Account Reconciliation Worksheet, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: Form 59E, Escrow Custodial Account Reconciliation Worksheet, are properly
elements.label: text

elements.text: prepared by and approved by different individuals reflecting the required segregation
elements.label: text

elements.text: of responsibilities
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Investor reporting performance standards
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will measure a Servicer’s investor reporting performance against certain
elements.label: text

elements.text: metrics, which may be amended by Freddie Mac from time to time.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Refer to the Servicer Success Scorecard for the specific metrics and to Section 3501.2
elements.label: text

elements.text: for additional information about the Servicer Success Scorecard.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 8301.2: Accounting and reporting methods, including
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: electronic formats, media, remitting requirements and
elements.label: text

elements.text: backup reporting (09/10/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains information related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Accounting reporting methods
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Electronic reporting format
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Reporting media
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Other reporting and remitting requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Backup reporting
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Accounting reporting methods
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must account for all Mortgages it services for Freddie Mac. Except in instances
elements.label: text

elements.text: noted in subsection (ii), Servicers must use the net yield accounting reporting method to pass
elements.label: text

elements.text: through principal and interest to Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Net yield
elements.label: text

elements.text: Under this method, the Servicer must report any principal payments collected in each
elements.label: text

elements.text: Accounting Cycle and the forecasted scheduled interest for the next Accounting Cycle
elements.label: text

elements.text: (forecasted scheduled interest and actual principal). Servicers must use the Accounting
elements.label: text

elements.text: Net Yield (ANY) to determine the amount of interest due to Freddie Mac for each
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage it services for Freddie Mac. The Servicer may have to advance its own funds to
elements.label: text

elements.text: ensure that Freddie Mac is able to draft the correct amount of interest.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Refer to Exhibit 60, Loan-Level Reporting Data Description, and Exhibit 61,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Interest and Principal Due Freddie Mac, for additional details regarding the net yield
elements.label: text

elements.text: accounting reporting method.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Alternate method
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac allows the Servicer to use the alternate method of reporting only if one of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the following conditions apply:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Applicable law prohibits the use of the net yield method
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Freddie Mac has instructed the Servicer to use the alternate method of reporting
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: The principal and interest the Servicer reports depends on actual payments collected
elements.label: text

elements.text: (actual interest and actual principal). If the Servicer does not collect interest, the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: does not report interest to Freddie Mac. If the Servicer collects payments for more than
elements.label: text

elements.text: one Accounting Cycle, the Servicer reports all of the interest collected and any applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: principal collected.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Refer to Exhibit 61 for interest calculation formulas used in the alternate method of
elements.label: text

elements.text: accounting reporting.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer uses the alternate method of accounting reporting and Freddie Mac has not
elements.label: text

elements.text: instructed the Servicer to do so, the Servicer must notify their Freddie Mac Investor
elements.label: text

elements.text: Reporting Representative in writing or Customer Service at 800-FREDDIE in advance of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Accounting Cycle reporting for that period. Otherwise, Freddie Mac will assume that
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Servicer is using the net yield interest method to report principal and interest to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Electronic reporting format
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac uses a common, industry-wide electronic data interchange (EDI) for the
elements.label: text

elements.text: investor reporting format. The American National Standards Institute’s (ANSI), Accredited
elements.label: text

elements.text: Standards Committee (ASC) is the nationally recognized governing body for the
elements.label: text

elements.text: development and maintenance of electronic data standards. Freddie Mac uses the
elements.label: text

elements.text: standardized investor reporting format known as the Investor Reporting (203) transaction set
elements.label: text

elements.text: that was developed by ASC.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer may or may not be required to use EDI depending on the Servicer’s selection of
elements.label: text

elements.text: a reporting medium as follows:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If the Servicer reports via Resolve® or the Freddie Mac Loan Level Reporting tool (see
elements.label: text

elements.text: Exhibit 88, Servicing Tools), it is not required to use the EDI format
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If the Servicer reports via another electronic communication path, as described in Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 8301.2(c)(2), it must use the EDI format
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If a service bureau reports data to Freddie Mac on a Servicer’s behalf, the service bureau
elements.label: text

elements.text: must use the EDI format
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Refer to the Investor Reporting EDI Implementation Guide or call Customer Service at
elements.label: text

elements.text: 800-FREDDIE for further information.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Reporting media
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac requires Servicers to use of one of the following methods to report activity on
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Mortgages serviced for Freddie Mac:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Resolve or the Freddie Mac Loan Level Reporting tool accessible via Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: Gateway (see Exhibit 88)
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Internet-based communications method (referred to as Generic Connect Services (GCS)).
elements.label: text

elements.text: GCS provides a variety of secure protocols that support a wide range of generic file
elements.label: text

elements.text: transfer situations, such as bulk file transmissions transmitted by service bureaus or
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers. Servicers that elect to use a service bureau to report activity on Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: serviced for Freddie Mac acknowledge and agree that Freddie Mac will send reports, data
elements.label: text

elements.text: and information on these Mortgages that will include, but not be limited to, the daily
elements.label: text

elements.text: Business-to-Business Response File and the Business-to-Business Draft File to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer’s service bureau.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In the event a Servicer changes its service bureau or begins to use a service bureau for
elements.label: text

elements.text: data reporting, it must notify Freddie Mac of the change in accordance with Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 8301.4(d) at least 45 days prior to the effective date of the change.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Investor accounting reports, such as the Servicer’s daily Edit Reports, Monthly Account
elements.label: text

elements.text: Statement, System Cleared Edits and Loan Reconciliation Difference Report are
elements.label: text

elements.text: available through the Loan Level Reporting tool.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer fails to comply with Freddie Mac’s reporting requirements, Freddie Mac will
elements.label: text

elements.text: assess a reporting noncompliance compensatory fee in accordance with the requirements of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Sections 8303.5(c) and 8303.5(d). See Section 8301.2(b) for additional information on
elements.label: text

elements.text: reporting formats.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For further information, call Customer Service at 800-FREDDIE.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Other reporting and remitting requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will draft the principal and interest for a repurchase in accordance with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements in Section 8303.3(f). The Servicer must transfer funds into the designated
elements.label: text

elements.text: Custodial Account prior to Freddie Mac’s draft of the repurchase proceeds.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Refer to Chapter 3602 for additional requirements for repurchases required by Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac or repurchases requested by the Servicer.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The following are default management procedures that have reporting and drafting
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements specified in other sections of the Guide:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Default management procedures
elements.label: text

elements.text: Topic Guide section
elements.label: text

elements.text: Repayment plans Section 9203.2(d)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Loan modifications Section 9206.4(c)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Short sales and make-whole preforeclosure sales Section 9208.3(a)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Deeds-in-lieu of foreclosure Section 9209.6
elements.label: text

elements.text: Charge-offs Section 9210.2
elements.label: text

elements.text: Third-party foreclosure sales Section 9301.9(c)
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e) Backup reporting
elements.label: text

elements.text: If Freddie Mac does not receive a loan-level transaction by the required reporting date for
elements.label: text

elements.text: any reason, the Servicer must be able to provide Freddie Mac (see Directory 7) with
elements.label: text

elements.text: corrected or replacement transmissions within 24 hours of Freddie Mac’s request, and the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer will be subject to accounting reporting noncompliance compensatory fees. Refer to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 8303 for noncompliance compensatory fees.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 8301.3: Calculating Mortgage interest due to Freddie
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Mac, net yield interest and Servicing fee (09/10/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Calculating Mortgage interest due to Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Net yield interest
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Servicing fee
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Calculating Mortgage interest due to Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: In the current Accounting Cycle, the Servicer must report the forecasted scheduled interest
elements.label: text

elements.text: for the next Accounting Cycle due to Freddie Mac. The next cycle forecasted scheduled
elements.label: text

elements.text: interest is calculated by multiplying Freddie Mac’s share of the current Accounting Cycle
elements.label: text

elements.text: interest-bearing Ending UPB, after applying all principal payments received from the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower, by the net yield divided by the applicable period of time. The Servicer must use
elements.label: text

elements.text: the following principles when calculating Mortgage interest due to Freddie Mac:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Forecasted scheduled interest is reported in the Accounting Cycle prior to the Accounting
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cycle it is due to Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Interest is due in arrears
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Freddie Mac will draft the forecasted scheduled interest in the Accounting Cycle after it
elements.label: text

elements.text: is reported
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Interest is due for full calendar months based on a 12-month, 360-day year
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Interest is due for partial months based on the actual number of days 365-day year
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ To calculate forecasted scheduled monthly interest for:
elements.label: text

elements.text:  Newly funded Mortgages, the Servicer must use the funded balance, which is the
elements.label: text

elements.text: beginning gross UPB
elements.label: text

elements.text:  For all other Mortgages, the Servicer must use either the current Accounting Cycle
elements.label: text

elements.text: Ending UPB or, in the case of a modified loan, the interest-bearing UPB reported at
elements.label: text

elements.text: the end of the current Accounting Cycle
elements.label: text

elements.text: For an ARM that allows Negative Amortization, the amount by which the monthly interest
elements.label: text

elements.text: calculated at the Note Rate exceeds the Borrower’s scheduled monthly payment to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer is a Negative Amortization increase and is accounted for in the calculation of
elements.label: text

elements.text: principal due to Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Net yield interest
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must calculate the interest due to Freddie Mac by using the Accounting Net
elements.label: text

elements.text: Yield (ANY) stated on Form 15, Loan Purchase Statement, and the Funding Detail Report or
elements.label: text

elements.text: as stated in the Transfer of Servicing records referenced in Section 7101.5
elements.label: text

elements.text: The yield is dependent upon the method of pricing chosen at the time the Mortgage was sold
elements.label: text

elements.text: to Freddie Mac. Generally, the yield equals the Note Rate minus the Servicing Spread. Refer
elements.label: text

elements.text: to Chapters 6101 and 6102 for pricing methods and product descriptions.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Servicing fee
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicing fee, referred to as the Servicing Spread, is the compensation the Servicer earns
elements.label: text

elements.text: for Servicing Freddie Mac-owned Mortgages.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Refer to Chapter 8105 for detailed information on Servicing fees and Section 8503.2(f) for
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicemembers Civil Relief Act-capped Mortgages.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 8301.4: Loan-level transaction, accounting cycle, cash
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: float, service bureau liability and change notification,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Custodial Accounts and required Servicer advances
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Loan-level transactions
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Accounting cycle
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Cash float
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Service bureau liability and notice of change of service bureau
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Administering Custodial Accounts
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Custodial Account reconciliation basics
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Servicer advances required
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Loan-level transactions
elements.label: text

elements.text: The term loan-level transaction refers to the required monthly and exception or liquidation
elements.label: text

elements.text: reporting of all activity that occurs during each Accounting Cycle for each Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: serviced for Freddie Mac. Freddie Mac considers a loan-level transaction to be reported when
elements.label: text

elements.text: it has been transmitted by the Servicer without errors and received by Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Daily reporting is encouraged but not required. Servicers must submit a loan-level
elements.label: text

elements.text: transaction for each Mortgage serviced for Freddie Mac no later than 2:00 a.m. Eastern Time
elements.label: text

elements.text: on the day following the P&I Determination Date. Servicers are required to report loan-level
elements.label: text

elements.text: activity from purchase date to liquidation. Refer to Exhibit 60, Loan-Level Reporting Data
elements.label: text

elements.text: Description. Refer to Section 8303.1(a) for the definition of exception (or liquidation)
elements.label: text

elements.text: activity.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Accounting Cycle
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Accounting Cycle will be the first day of the calendar month through the last day of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: calendar month. Servicer reporting for the Accounting Cycle must be completed no later than
elements.label: text

elements.text: the last day of the calendar month plus one Business Day.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Cash float
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac does not draft monthly principal and interest until the P&I Draft Date. The
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer may earn interest on the cash float of funds it deposits into the Custodial Accounts.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Refer to Section 8304.2(a) for Freddie Mac’s requirements regarding the investment of funds
elements.label: text

elements.text: in the Custodial Accounts.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Service bureau liability and notice of change of service bureau
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer uses a service bureau for data processing, the Servicer remains responsible for
elements.label: text

elements.text: all obligations to Freddie Mac under the Purchase Documents (see Section 8102.1(e)). The
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer must pay any noncompliance compensatory fees, even though it may be the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer’s service bureau’s error that causes the noncompliance.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers that elect to use a service bureau to report activity on Mortgages serviced for
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac acknowledge and agree that Freddie Mac will send reports, data and
elements.label: text

elements.text: information on these Mortgages that will include, but not be limited to, the daily Business-to￾Business Response File and the Business-to-Business Draft File to the Servicer’s service
elements.label: text

elements.text: bureau.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a Servicer changes its service bureau or begins to use a new service bureau for data
elements.label: text

elements.text: reporting or would like Freddie Mac to send these files to a specific service bureau, it must
elements.label: text

elements.text: notify Freddie Mac at least 45 days prior to the effective date of the change. The Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: must provide this notification via e-mail
elements.label: text

elements.text: (Servicing_Ops_App_Support_IR@FreddieMac.com) and include the Seller/Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: number(s), point of contact, phone number, current and future service bureau with its contact
elements.label: text

elements.text: information and effective date of the change. Servicers acknowledge that the notification of a
elements.label: text

elements.text: change of service bureau is necessary for Freddie Mac to provide reports, edits and feedback
elements.label: text

elements.text: to the correct service bureau.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e) Administering Custodial Accounts
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer is responsible for administering Custodial Accounts to safeguard funds due to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac. In addition, the Servicer must ensure that each account is adequately funded
elements.label: text

elements.text: and reconciled monthly. Refer to Chapter 8302 for requirements on establishing Custodial
elements.label: text

elements.text: Accounts and to Chapter 8304 for requirements on administering and reconciling the cash in
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Custodial Accounts.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (f) Custodial Account reconciliation basics
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must reconcile all Custodial Accounts monthly as of the end of the Accounting
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cycle. A Custodial Account reconciliation compares the funds on deposit as of the end of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Accounting Cycle to the total liability as of the same date. Any differences between any two
elements.label: text

elements.text: parts of the investor accounting process may cause a Custodial Account to be out of balance.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Refer to Chapter 8304 for Freddie Mac’s requirements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (g) Servicer advances required
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must advance its funds into the Principal and Interest Custodial Account if:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ There are insufficient funds to cover the draft amount due to Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Servicer is completing a Mortgage repurchase
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Servicer has an unidentified and unresolved variance on the Custodial Account
elements.label: text

elements.text: reconciliation resulting in the account being underfunded (see Chapter 8304)
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Must make any advances for funds due to Freddie Mac prior to the P&I Draft Date and/or
elements.label: text

elements.text: the applicable Payoff Draft Date
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Must make a deposit advanced from its funds into the Escrow Custodial Account
elements.label: text

elements.text: whenever a deficiency occurs in an individual Escrow account
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Must not use the Escrow collections from one Mortgage to offset the deficiency of
elements.label: text

elements.text: another
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ May not carry a negative balance in either the Principal and Interest Custodial Account or
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Escrow Custodial Account even if permitted by its depository institution
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer may reimburse itself for:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Advances of Principal and Interest Payments upon subsequent receipt of any Principal
elements.label: text

elements.text: and Interest Payments
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Advances of Escrow from Escrow Funds received for the Mortgage for which the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer made the advance
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 8302: Investor Reporting and Remitting
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Functions
elements.label: text

elements.text: 8302.1: Custodial Account requirements and
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: management (10/08/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains information related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Custodial Accounts required
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Opening Custodial Accounts
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Custodial Account and custodial clearing account designation requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Custodial Account documentation requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ What funds to deposit to the Custodial Accounts
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ When to deposit funds to the Custodial Account
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Administering and reconciling Custodial Accounts
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Honoring sight drafts
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Custodial Accounts required
elements.label: text

elements.text: For all Mortgages serviced for Freddie Mac, the Servicer must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Open and maintain a Custodial Account for Principal and Interest Payments and a
elements.label: text

elements.text: separate Custodial Account for Escrow Funds for each Seller/Servicer number
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Open these accounts in accordance with the requirements of this section and use these
elements.label: text

elements.text: accounts exclusively for the Mortgages it services for Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Hold any funds the Servicer receives for Freddie Mac-owned Mortgages in a Custodial
elements.label: text

elements.text: Account at all times
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Ensure that the accounts are adequately funded at all times and do not carry a negative
elements.label: text

elements.text: balance, even if permitted by the depository institution
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Maintain these funds in their respective Custodial Accounts until Freddie Mac drafts the
elements.label: text

elements.text: funds or the Servicer pays for allowed Mortgage expenses with the Escrow Funds in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Escrow Custodial Account
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Principal and Interest Custodial Account
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must open a Principal and Interest Custodial Account for each
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer number to deposit Principal and Interest Payments solely for the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages the Servicer services for Freddie Mac. The Servicer has the option to open
elements.label: text

elements.text: more than one Principal and Interest Custodial Account. If the Servicer opens more
elements.label: text

elements.text: than one Principal and Interest Custodial Account, the Servicer must designate one of
elements.label: text

elements.text: them as the primary account from which Freddie Mac will draft principal and interest
elements.label: text

elements.text: payments and payoffs. Alternatively, the Servicer may open a separate Principal and
elements.label: text

elements.text: Interest Disbursement Clearing Custodial Account. Refer to Section 8302.2(a) for
elements.label: text

elements.text: Principal and Interest Disbursement Clearing Account requirements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: See Section 8304.2(b) for information on maintaining the Principal and Interest
elements.label: text

elements.text: Custodial Account.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Escrow Custodial Account
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must hold Escrow Funds in a separate Escrow Custodial Account for
elements.label: text

elements.text: each Seller/Servicer number. Even if the Servicer does not collect Escrow Funds, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer must open an Escrow Custodial Account in the event that the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: receives funds for insurance claims proceeds, partial payments or buydown funds. In
elements.label: text

elements.text: addition, although Freddie Mac allows the Servicer to hold buydown funds in an
elements.label: text

elements.text: Escrow Custodial Account, the Servicer has the option to open a separate Buydown
elements.label: text

elements.text: Custodial Account for buydown funds.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: See Section 8304.2(c) for information on maintaining the Escrow Custodial
elements.label: text

elements.text: Account.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Opening Custodial Accounts
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must open and maintain all Custodial Accounts at an Eligible Depository.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer’s institution is an Eligible Depository that meets the requirements contained in
elements.label: text

elements.text: this chapter, the Servicer may open and maintain Custodial Accounts within its own financial
elements.label: text

elements.text: institution. The Servicer must indemnify Freddie Mac for and hold it harmless from any loss,
elements.label: text

elements.text: damage or expenses (including court costs and attorney fees) that Freddie Mac sustains as a
elements.label: text

elements.text: direct or indirect result of the financial institution’s bankruptcy, receivership, liquidation or
elements.label: text

elements.text: insolvency.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Refer to Sections 8302.2(b) and 8302.2(c) for Eligible Depositories.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer agrees to safeguard at all times the funds it maintains in Custodial Accounts on
elements.label: text

elements.text: behalf of Freddie Mac. Even if the Servicer has complied with all Guide and Purchase
elements.label: text

elements.text: Document requirements, Servicer agrees to indemnify and hold Freddie Mac harmless for
elements.label: text

elements.text: any loss of funds deposited in a Custodial Account and any damages Freddie Mac suffers
elements.label: text

elements.text: because of delays in obtaining the funds from a Custodial Account.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer itself is a depository institution that meets the requirements contained in this
elements.label: text

elements.text: chapter for an Eligible Depository or the Servicer is a subsidiary of or under common
elements.label: text

elements.text: ownership of such a depository institution, the Servicer may open and maintain a custodial
elements.label: text

elements.text: clearing account and Custodial Accounts within such depository institution.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer may initially deposit Freddie Mac funds into one or more custodial clearing
elements.label: text

elements.text: accounts prior to transferring such funds to the appropriate Custodial Account, and the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer may record that initial deposit in a general ledger account or internal operating
elements.label: text

elements.text: account for record-keeping purposes, provided that:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Servicer opens separate Custodial Accounts as otherwise required under the Guide
elements.label: text

elements.text: and applicable Purchase Documents
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The institution at which any custodial clearing accounts are maintained meets the
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements to be an Eligible Depository
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The custodial clearing account is titled in accordance with Section 8302.1(c)(v); and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Servicer and the institution maintain adequate records and audit trails to support all
elements.label: text

elements.text: credits to and charges from the Borrower’s payment records and the custodial clearing
elements.label: text

elements.text: accounts and separate Custodial Accounts
elements.label: text

elements.text: Each Custodial Account must be a Demand Deposit Account or Interest-Bearing Deposit
elements.label: text

elements.text: Account. The Servicer’s use of an Interest-Bearing Deposit Account is conditioned upon
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac’s ability to present a sight draft and draft monthly principal and interest
elements.label: text

elements.text: payments and payoffs when due. If a Custodial Account limits the number of withdrawals or
elements.label: text

elements.text: requires prior notice for withdrawals, the Servicer is responsible for any payments or
elements.label: text

elements.text: penalties that result from excessive withdrawals or withdrawals where advance notice is
elements.label: text

elements.text: insufficient.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must maintain its books and records in good faith and in the regular course of
elements.label: text

elements.text: business, and the Custodial Accounts and the funds held therein must be free and clear of any
elements.label: text

elements.text: and all liens or claims of others, including security agreements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Custodial Accounts are maintained in an Eligible Depository that is an FDIC-Insured
elements.label: text

elements.text: Depository or an NCUSIF-Insured Depository, then:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Servicer’s books and records must reflect that the funds in the Custodial Accounts
elements.label: text

elements.text: are held in a custodial capacity for the benefit of Freddie Mac and/or other specified
elements.label: text

elements.text: parties and must identify the respective interests of all such persons
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Servicer must maintain the Custodial Accounts and its books and records in
elements.label: text

elements.text: accordance with all requirements for full federal insurance coverage
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Custodial Accounts must permit Freddie Mac to directly draft funds for principal and
elements.label: text

elements.text: interest payments and payoffs.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Custodial Account and custodial clearing account designation requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must designate Custodial Accounts exactly as stated in subsections (i) through
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iv) below and custodial clearing accounts stated in subsection (v) below. If the Servicer has
elements.label: text

elements.text: an approved Servicing Agent relationship, the Subservicer (Servicing Agent) name must be
elements.label: text

elements.text: captured within the account designation along with the Servicer’s name. The Servicer’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: books and records pertaining to Custodial Accounts and custodial clearing accounts must
elements.label: text

elements.text: comply with these account designation requirements at all times. The Servicer represents and
elements.label: text

elements.text: warrants to and covenants and agrees with Freddie Mac that all books and records of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Eligible Depository pertaining to such Custodial Accounts and custodial clearing
elements.label: text

elements.text: accounts shall at all times comply with requirements in subsections (i) through (v) below.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Principal and Interest Custodial Accounts
elements.label: text

elements.text: The following account designation must be used: “(Name of Depositor/Servicer), as
elements.label: text

elements.text: Custodian and/or bailee for Federal Home Loan Mortgage Corporation and/or various
elements.label: text

elements.text: owners of interests in mortgages and/or mortgage-related securities and/or various
elements.label: text

elements.text: mortgagors.”
elements.label: text

elements.text: Alternatively, a Principal and Interest Custodial Account may be designated by the
elements.label: text

elements.text: following abbreviated designation: “(Name of Depositor/Servicer), Freddie Mac P &
elements.label: text

elements.text: I Custodial Account.” However, if the abbreviated designation is used, then for all
elements.label: text

elements.text: purposes of the Purchase Documents and with respect to all rights and interests of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac and/or various owners of interests in Mortgages and/or Mortgage-related
elements.label: text

elements.text: securities and/or mortgagors, the abbreviated account designation shall be deemed to be
elements.label: text

elements.text: the same as the unabbreviated account designation and shall be deemed to confer upon
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac and such persons the same rights and interests with respect to the Principal
elements.label: text

elements.text: and Interest Custodial Account and the funds deposited or held therein.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Principal and Interest Disbursement Clearing Custodial Accounts
elements.label: text

elements.text: The following account designation must be used: “(Name of Depositor/Servicer), as
elements.label: text

elements.text: custodian and/or bailee for Federal Home Loan Mortgage Corporation and/or various
elements.label: text

elements.text: owners of interests in mortgages and/or mortgage-related securities and/or various
elements.label: text

elements.text: mortgagors.”
elements.label: text

elements.text: Alternatively, a Principal and Interest Disbursement Clearing Custodial Account may be
elements.label: text

elements.text: designated by the following abbreviated designation: “(Name of Depositor/Servicer),
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac P & I Clearing Custodial Account.” However, if the abbreviated designation
elements.label: text

elements.text: is used, then for all purposes of the Purchase Documents and with respect to all rights and
elements.label: text

elements.text: interests of Freddie Mac and/or various owners of interests in Mortgages and/or
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage-related securities and/or mortgagors, the abbreviated account designation shall
elements.label: text

elements.text: be deemed to be the same as the unabbreviated account designation and shall be deemed
elements.label: text

elements.text: to confer upon Freddie Mac and such persons the same rights and interests with respect to
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Principal and Interest Disbursement Clearing Custodial Account and the funds
elements.label: text

elements.text: deposited or held therein.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii) Escrow Custodial Accounts
elements.label: text

elements.text: The following account designation must be used: “(Name of Depositor/Servicer), as
elements.label: text

elements.text: trustee and/or custodian for Federal Home Loan Mortgage Corporation and various
elements.label: text

elements.text: mortgagors, respectively.”
elements.label: text

elements.text: Alternatively, an Escrow Custodial Account may be designated by the following
elements.label: text

elements.text: abbreviated designation: “(Name of Depositor/Servicer), Freddie Mac Escrow Custodial
elements.label: text

elements.text: Account.” However, if the abbreviated designation is used, then for all purposes of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Purchase Documents and with respect to all rights and interests of Freddie Mac and/or
elements.label: text

elements.text: mortgagors, the abbreviated account designation shall be deemed to be the same as the
elements.label: text

elements.text: unabbreviated account designation and shall be deemed to confer upon Freddie Mac and
elements.label: text

elements.text: such persons the same rights and interests with respect to the Escrow Custodial
elements.label: text

elements.text: Account and the funds deposited or held therein.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iv) Buydown Custodial Accounts
elements.label: text

elements.text: If an optional Buydown Custodial Account is used in accordance with the provisions of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 8302.1(a)(ii), the following account designation must be used: “(Name of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Depositor/Servicer), as trustee and/or custodian of mortgage loan subsidy buydown funds
elements.label: text

elements.text: for Federal Home Loan Mortgage Corporation and various mortgagors, respectively.”
elements.label: text

elements.text: Alternatively, a Buydown Custodial Account may be designated by the following
elements.label: text

elements.text: abbreviated designation: “(Name of Depositor/Servicer), Freddie Mac Buydown
elements.label: text

elements.text: Custodial Account.” However, if the abbreviated designation is used, then for all
elements.label: text

elements.text: purposes of the Purchase Documents and with respect to all rights and interests of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac and/or mortgagors, the abbreviated account designation shall be deemed to
elements.label: text

elements.text: be the same as the unabbreviated account designation and shall be deemed to confer upon
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac and such persons the same rights and interests with respect to the Buydown
elements.label: text

elements.text: Custodial Account and the funds deposited or held therein.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (v) Custodial clearing accounts
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers using custodial clearing accounts must title the account to indicate it is
elements.label: text

elements.text: custodial in nature and include at a minimum “for the benefit of the Federal Home Loan
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage Corporation” in the account title.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Custodial Account documentation requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must submit the following documentation to Freddie Mac upon opening the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer’s Custodial Accounts at an Eligible Depository. If the Servicer has an approved
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing Agent relationship, the letter agreements should be completed by the Servicer or
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing Agent that opened the Fund Custodian accounts on behalf of the Servicer. The
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer Tax ID information provided on the letter agreement would depend on whether the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer or Servicing Agent opened the Fund Custodian accounts. Freddie Mac bases the
elements.label: text

elements.text: required documentation on whether the Servicer opens the accounts at its own financial
elements.label: text

elements.text: institution or at another Eligible Depository.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Custodial Accounts opened at the Servicer’s own financial institution
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer is an Eligible Depository and opens Custodial Accounts at its own
elements.label: text

elements.text: financial institution, the Servicer must complete, execute and return the following
elements.label: text

elements.text: documentation to Freddie Mac (see Directory 1) for each Custodial Account:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Form 1059SF, Letter Agreement for Single-Family Principal and Interest, or P & I
elements.label: text

elements.text: Disbursement Clearing Custodial Account
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Form 1060SF, Letter Agreement for Single-Family Buydown or Escrow Custodial
elements.label: text

elements.text: Account
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Form 1060CR, Letter Agreement for Single-Family Custodial Account for
elements.label: text

elements.text: Renovation Funds
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must also send Freddie Mac a copy of the bank statement or signature card
elements.label: text

elements.text: for each account, designated as required in Section 8302.1(c). The documentation must
elements.label: text

elements.text: identify the Eligible Depository at which the account is maintained and the bank account
elements.label: text

elements.text: number.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Custodial Accounts opened at another Eligible Depository
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer opens Custodial Accounts at an Eligible Depository other than its own
elements.label: text

elements.text: financial institution, both the Servicer and the depository’s representative must complete,
elements.label: text

elements.text: execute and return the following documentation to Freddie Mac (see Directory 1) for
elements.label: text

elements.text: each Custodial Account:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Form 1057SF, Letter Agreement for Servicer’s Single-Family Principal and Interest,
elements.label: text

elements.text: or P & I Disbursement Clearing Custodial Account
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Form 1058SF, Letter Agreement for Servicer’s Single-Family Buydown or Escrow
elements.label: text

elements.text: Custodial Account
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Form 1058CR, Letter Agreement for Seller/Servicer’s Single-Family Custodial
elements.label: text

elements.text: Account for Renovation Funds
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must also send Freddie Mac a copy of the bank statement or signature card
elements.label: text

elements.text: for each account, designated as required in Section 8302.1(c). The documentation must
elements.label: text

elements.text: identify the Eligible Depository at which the account is maintained and the bank account
elements.label: text

elements.text: number.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii) Additional requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: For each Seller/Servicer number for the Principal and Interest Custodial Account or
elements.label: text

elements.text: Principal and Interest Disbursement Clearing Custodial Account the Servicer selects as
elements.label: text

elements.text: its remittance drafting account according to the requirements of Sections 8302.1(a)(i) and
elements.label: text

elements.text: 8302.2(a)(i), Freddie Mac requires the Servicer to submit a copy of the Letter of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Authorization (Exhibit 58, Draft Letter of Authorization), printed on the Servicer’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: institution’s letterhead, to Freddie Mac (see Directory 1). The sample letter included in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Exhibit 58 provides the Servicer with the information that must be included in the letter
elements.label: text

elements.text: of authorization. Even if the Servicer opens its Custodial Account at its own financial
elements.label: text

elements.text: institution, the Servicer must execute this letter. The depository where the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: opened and maintains the account must keep a copy of this letter.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e) What funds to deposit to the Custodial Accounts
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must deposit the following funds for Mortgages in which Freddie Mac holds an
elements.label: text

elements.text: ownership interest to a Principal and Interest Custodial Account or an Escrow Custodial
elements.label: text

elements.text: Account. The Servicer must not deposit funds from Mortgages that Freddie Mac does not
elements.label: text

elements.text: own or from other investors into the Custodial Accounts that the Servicer maintains to hold
elements.label: text

elements.text: funds for Freddie Mac’s Mortgages.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Principal and Interest Custodial Accounts
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must deposit only funds due to Freddie Mac into a Principal and Interest
elements.label: text

elements.text: Custodial Account. As an exception, the Servicer may deposit the full Principal and
elements.label: text

elements.text: Interest Payment received into the Principal and Interest Custodial Account and
elements.label: text

elements.text: subsequently withdraw its Servicing fee. Following is a list of the funds the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: must deposit into the Principal and Interest Custodial Account for Freddie Mac’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Principal and Interest Payments on all of Freddie Mac’s Mortgages whether received
elements.label: text

elements.text: from the Borrower or paid on the Borrower’s behalf, applied from the buydown
elements.label: text

elements.text: account or advanced by the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Biweekly payments that require biweekly amortization based on the Note terms of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages originally sold to Freddie Mac as biweekly
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. Principal curtailments
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. Payoff proceeds, including those from short sales and third-party foreclosure sales
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5. Prepayment penalties, if applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: 6. Repurchase proceeds
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Escrow Custodial Accounts
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must deposit all Escrow Funds for Freddie Mac’s Mortgages into an
elements.label: text

elements.text: Escrow Custodial Account opened and maintained according to Freddie Mac’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements. Following is a list of the funds that the Servicer must deposit into the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Escrow Custodial Account for Freddie Mac’s Mortgages:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. All Escrow Funds paid by or on behalf of the Borrower or advanced by the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Funds deposited by the Servicer on a Borrower’s behalf such as property insurance
elements.label: text

elements.text: claim proceeds
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. Payments held as unapplied or in suspense pending proper distribution such as partial
elements.label: text

elements.text: payments and biweekly payments from plans that allow for biweekly collection of
elements.label: text

elements.text: payments on monthly amortizing Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. Buydown funds scheduled for future application as part of a Borrower’s Principal and
elements.label: text

elements.text: Interest Payment. The Servicer may also choose the option of maintaining buydown
elements.label: text

elements.text: funds separately as provided in Section 8302.1(a)(ii).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (f) When to deposit funds to the Custodial Account
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers that use a custodial clearing account must deposit all funds the Servicer receives for
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac’s Mortgages into the custodial clearing accounts on the day the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: receives the funds.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers must deposit all funds the Servicer receives for Freddie Mac’s Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: into Custodial Accounts no later than the first Business Day after the Servicer receives them.
elements.label: text

elements.text: However, if the Servicer uses a lockbox service or other service to collect payments, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer must apply the payment and deposit the funds to Freddie Mac’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: corresponding Custodial Accounts no later than the second Business Day after the day on
elements.label: text

elements.text: which the lockbox or other service received the payment.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Other than a Borrower’s payment, the Servicer must make all additional required deposits to
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Custodial Accounts as referenced in Section 8302.1(e) so the funds are available by the
elements.label: text

elements.text: remittance drafting or Escrow disbursement due date.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (g) Administering and reconciling Custodial Accounts
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must reconcile all of Freddie Mac’s Custodial Accounts on a monthly basis.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Refer to Chapter 8304 for Freddie Mac’s administration and reconciliation requirements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (h) Honoring sight drafts
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Eligible Depository the Servicer selects must honor sight drafts Freddie Mac may
elements.label: text

elements.text: present at any time against any Custodial Account that the Servicer maintains. Freddie Mac’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: authorized officer, employee, representative, agent or attorney must sign the sight draft.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will identify the authorized individual in Form 1061, Certificate of Incumbency
elements.label: text

elements.text: and Authority to Draft Against Custodial Accounts, or a similar form containing identical
elements.label: text

elements.text: information, including a photocopy or facsimile copy.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The sight draft Freddie Mac presents may be an original; a photographic or facsimile copy of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Form 1062, Sight Draft; or a similar form containing identical information. Honoring such a
elements.label: text

elements.text: sight draft is one of the requirements in the letter agreements referenced in Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 8302.2: Depository and Custodial Account
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: requirements (09/10/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains information related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Disbursement clearing accounts
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Depository eligibility requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Insured depository risk level
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Changing or transferring the drafting account
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Disbursement clearing accounts
elements.label: text

elements.text: A disbursement clearing account is a bank account the Servicer maintains for the deposit of
elements.label: text

elements.text: funds due to Freddie Mac or paid to third parties, such as insurance premiums or property
elements.label: text

elements.text: taxes.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Principal and Interest Disbursement Clearing Custodial Account
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Principal and Interest Disbursement Clearing Custodial Account is only necessary if
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Servicer chooses to maintain the account from which Freddie Mac drafts funds as a
elements.label: text

elements.text: separate account. The Servicer may not clear Freddie Mac’s funds through an operating
elements.label: text

elements.text: account or a general account through which the Servicer processes non-Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: Automated Clearing House transfers.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer chooses to open and use a Principal and Interest Disbursement Clearing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Custodial Account, the Servicer must meet the following requirements:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Open, maintain and designate the account in accordance with the requirements of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Sections 8302.1(b) through 8302.1(d), 8302.2(b) and 8302.2(c)
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Deposit only funds due to Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. Ensure that the disbursement clearing account is adequately funded prior to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable draft date
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Escrow Disbursement Clearing Account
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac does not require the Servicer to maintain a separate Escrow Disbursement
elements.label: text

elements.text: Clearing Account for Escrow Funds. However, if the Servicer elects to deposit Escrow
elements.label: text

elements.text: Funds into an Escrow Disbursement Clearing Account, the Servicer:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ May deposit Escrow Funds for Freddie Mac’s Mortgages into an Escrow
elements.label: text

elements.text: Disbursement Clearing Account common to all Mortgages the Servicer services
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Must ensure that it transfers the Escrow Funds from the Escrow Custodial Account to
elements.label: text

elements.text: its Escrow Disbursement Clearing Account no more than one Business Day prior to
elements.label: text

elements.text: the disbursement of any Escrow Funds
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Must ensure that the Escrow Disbursement Clearing Account is adequately funded
elements.label: text

elements.text: prior to the disbursement of any Escrow Funds
elements.label: text

elements.text: An Escrow Disbursement Clearing Account is not subject to Custodial Account
elements.label: text

elements.text: designation or documentation requirements, as set forth in Sections 8302.1(c) and
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Depository eligibility requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: To ensure the safety of funds in Custodial Accounts, Freddie Mac has specific eligibility
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements that a depository must meet.
elements.label: text

elements.text: An Eligible Depository for the Custodial Accounts must be one of the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A Federal Reserve Bank
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A Federal Home Loan Bank
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ An FDIC-Insured Depository
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ An NCUSIF-Insured Depository
elements.label: text

elements.text: An FDIC-Insured Depository or an NCUSIF-Insured Depository must also meet Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac’s acceptable risk threshold and minimum tier rating requirements specified in Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: Depositories for Escrow and Buydown Custodial Accounts must meet Freddie Mac’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: depository eligibility requirements and are also required to meet Freddie Mac’s acceptable
elements.label: text

elements.text: risk threshold as stated in Section 8302.2(c).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Insured depository risk level
elements.label: text

elements.text: An FDIC-Insured Depository or an NCUSIF-Insured Depository for Principal and Interest
elements.label: text

elements.text: Custodial Accounts must maintain an acceptable risk threshold based upon Freddie Mac’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: confidential tolerance standards calculated using various key metrics. Servicers will be
elements.label: text

elements.text: notified if its depository has fallen below the acceptable risk threshold.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In addition to the acceptable risk threshold, an FDIC-Insured Depository or an NCUSIF￾Insured Depository must meet the following requirements:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Be rated as “well capitalized” by its federal or State regulator; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Have a financial rating that meets at least one of the following criteria:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Institutions with assets of $30 billion or more must have either a:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Short-term issuer rating by S&P of “A-3” (or better) or, if no short-term issuer
elements.label: text

elements.text: rating is available by S&P, a long-term issuer rating of “BBB-” (or better) by
elements.label: text

elements.text: S&P; or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Short-term bank deposit rating by Moody’s of “P-3” (or better) or, if no short￾term bank deposit rating is available by Moody’s, a long-term bank deposit rating
elements.label: text

elements.text: of “Baa3” (or better) by Moody’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Institutions with assets of less than $30 billion must have either a:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ 125 (or better) Bank safety rating issued by IDC Financial Publishing, Inc.; or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ C+ (or better) KBRA Financial Intelligence (KFI) Score issued by Kroll Bond
elements.label: text

elements.text: Rating Agency, LLC
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must monitor these ratings based on the frequency used by the ratings
elements.label: text

elements.text: agency for publishing and updating rating changes to determine the continued eligibility
elements.label: text

elements.text: of a depository institution.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac may require that funds be transferred out of an Eligible Depository—even if the
elements.label: text

elements.text: institution satisfies Freddie Mac’s financial rating criteria—or more quickly than indicated
elements.label: text

elements.text: above if Freddie Mac decides that it is in its best interests or the interests of PC, MBS and/or
elements.label: text

elements.text: UMBS® investors to do so.
elements.label: text

elements.text: A depository institution that is a subsidiary of a holding company or an affiliate of another
elements.label: text

elements.text: depository may, on a case-by-case basis, have its rating affected by the rating of its holding
elements.label: text

elements.text: company or affiliate. FDIC regulations may require related depository institutions to
elements.label: text

elements.text: guarantee the obligations of the troubled depository institutions. As a result, the related
elements.label: text

elements.text: depository institution may be subject to a risk of regulatory action, and Freddie Mac may
elements.label: text

elements.text: deem it to be ineligible even if it meets Freddie Mac’s acceptable risk level. The Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: may want to consider this when selecting or monitoring its depository.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Changing or transferring the drafting account
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Servicer must comply with the requirements in Sections 8304.3(b), (c) and (d) in order to
elements.label: text

elements.text: change or transfer the Principal and Interest Custodial Account from which Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: drafts its monthly principal and interest payments and payoff amounts.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer may also visit Freddie Mac Learning at https://sf.freddiemac.com/tools￾learning/freddie-mac-learning/overview to access training resources or call Customer
elements.label: text

elements.text: Service at 800-FREDDIE.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 8302.3: Investor reporting and remitting (10/08/25)

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:

elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Transmitting and receiving investor accounting information
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac requires the Servicer to submit all reports to Freddie Mac via one of Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac’s automated systems. The Servicer’s portfolio size and available technology will help
elements.label: text

elements.text: determine which system or combination of systems the Servicer will need.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Below are the methods the Servicer may use to transmit loan-level data to Freddie Mac or to
elements.label: text

elements.text: receive certain information from Freddie Mac:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Methods available to transmit loan-level data to Freddie Mac

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: (available through Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: Gateway) (see Exhibit 88, Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Tools)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Internet-based transmission (GCS)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Other Freddie Mac-approved reporting
elements.label: text

elements.text: method
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Loan Level Reporting and Resolve
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Internet-based transmission (GCS)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Receive reports and communications from
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac
