elements.label: text

elements.text: Receive purchase statements and funding
elements.label: text

elements.text: detail reports
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Loan Selling Advisor®
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac provides the Investor Reporting EDI Implementation Guide as an additional
elements.label: text

elements.text: resource related to investor accounting.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer may also visit Freddie Mac Learning at https://sf.freddiemac.com/tools￾learning/freddie-mac-learning/overview to access training resources or call Customer
elements.label: text

elements.text: Service at 800-FREDDIE.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Pre-purchase activity
elements.label: text

elements.text: Refer to the following chapters for Freddie Mac’s Mortgage processing and purchase
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac’s Mortgage processing and purchase requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: Guide topic Guide location
elements.label: text

elements.text: Fixed-rate Cash Chapter 6101
elements.label: text

elements.text: ARM Cash Chapter 6102
elements.label: text

elements.text: Documentation delivery Chapter 6301
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage delivery instructions Chapter 6302
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) When Freddie Mac purchases a Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Purchase of a Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: When Freddie Mac purchases a Mortgage, it will:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Make the following forms and data available via Loan Selling Advisor:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Form 15/A/C, Loan Purchase Statement
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Funding Detail Report
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Wire the Purchase Proceeds to the Seller for a cash Purchase Contract or issue a
elements.label: text

elements.text: security (UMBS, MBS or WAC ARM PC) for Guarantor or MultiLender Swap
elements.label: text

elements.text: programs
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Loan Purchase Statement
elements.label: text

elements.text: Upon purchase, Freddie Mac will make available in Loan Selling Advisor Form 15/A/C,
elements.label: text

elements.text: which will summarize the pertinent data. Form 15/A/C will include a computation of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: amount to be funded to the Seller.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Depending on the type of purchase, the Seller may access one of the following forms in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Loan Selling Advisor:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Loan Purchase Statement for Cash Sales
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Form 15A for fixed-rate Guarantor
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. Form 15C for WAC ARM Guarantor
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii) Funding Detail Report
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will make available to the Seller/Servicer a Funding Detail Report via Loan
elements.label: text

elements.text: Selling Advisor when Freddie Mac purchases a Mortgage or when the Servicer assumes
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Servicing Contract and related Servicing Contract Rights related to one or more of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac’s Mortgages through a Concurrent Transfer of Servicing. (See Chapter 7101
elements.label: text

elements.text: for information on Transfers of Servicing.) The Funding Detail Report contains all of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: information the Servicer needs to set up and report a Mortgage (see Section 8302.3(d)).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iv) Wiring the Purchase Proceeds or issuing a UMBS, MBS, WAC or ARM PC
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will notify the Seller of Freddie Mac’s purchase and initiate a wire transfer
elements.label: text

elements.text: to credit the Seller’s account on the Funding Date for Cash Purchase programs. See
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 2201.2 for additional information regarding establishing and maintaining wire
elements.label: text

elements.text: transfer instructions for cash proceeds. For Guarantor or certain MultiLender Swaps,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will issue a UMBS, MBS or WAC ARM PC; for other MultiLender Swaps,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will issue a Super (see Section 6201.1 for more information).
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller is responsible for verifying the receipt of funds. If the Seller finds that the
elements.label: text

elements.text: wired funds are not credited to the account by the morning of the next Business Day, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller must contact Customer Service at 800-FREDDIE.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must not use the Freddie Mac Custodial Account to receive sales proceeds
elements.label: text

elements.text: for Mortgages sold to Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Setting up a Mortgage record
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller/Servicer may access Form 15/A/C and the Funding Detail Report through Loan
elements.label: text

elements.text: Selling Advisor. (Call Customer Service at 800-FREDDIE to request access to Loan Selling
elements.label: text

elements.text: Advisor for this purpose.) The Servicer must verify the data on the Funding Detail Report to
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Note, Mortgage record and Purchase Documents and take the following actions for each
elements.label: text

elements.text: newly funded Mortgage, including those related to a Concurrent Transfer of Servicing. For
elements.label: text

elements.text: Subsequent Transfers of Servicing, the Transferor Servicer must supply the loan-level data to
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Transferee Servicer.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Upon Freddie Mac’s purchase, prompt identification of the accounting records for Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: sold to Freddie Mac is essential for accurate reporting and remitting. The Servicer must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Identify on its Servicing system Mortgages in which Freddie Mac has an ownership
elements.label: text

elements.text: interest
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Deposit any principal and interest and Escrow Funds to the Custodial Accounts
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. Maintain the Mortgage files in accordance with the requirements of Section 8101.4(c)
elements.label: text

elements.text: and Chapter 3302
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: The Freddie Mac Loan Level Reporting tool (see Exhibit 88, Servicing Tools) imports
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage files daily. If a Mortgage record is missing from the Freddie Mac Service Loans
elements.label: text

elements.text: application, the Servicer should contact its investor reporting specialist or call Customer
elements.label: text

elements.text: Service at 800-FREDDIE for assistance in setting up the Mortgage record.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e) Purchase adjustments
elements.label: text

elements.text: When the information on the Funding Detail Report does not match the information on the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note or the Seller’s loan record, the Seller may request an adjustment to the amount Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac disbursed to purchase the Mortgage. The Seller must notify Freddie Mac in writing and
elements.label: text

elements.text: include supporting documentation.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In most circumstances Freddie Mac will not adjust purchase balances after the Funding Date
elements.label: text

elements.text: because Freddie Mac has pooled the Mortgages for resale as UMBS, MBS or WAC ARM
elements.label: text

elements.text: PC. However, after Freddie Mac settles and before it pools the Mortgages, Freddie Mac may
elements.label: text

elements.text: be able to adjust a purchase balance.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (f) Reporting and remitting on a newly funded Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Mortgage balance and the timing of the Funding Date determine when the Servicer must
elements.label: text

elements.text: report for the first time on a newly funded Mortgage. The Servicer must report on the newly
elements.label: text

elements.text: funded Mortgage according to its Purchase Documents. When Freddie Mac purchases a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage, Freddie Mac will pay the Seller interest on the funded principal balance at the net
elements.label: text

elements.text: yield from the first of the month up to the Funding Date. The Servicer must ensure that it
elements.label: text

elements.text: properly funds its Principal and Interest Custodial Account for this interest. The following

elements.text: Chapter 8303: Reporting and Drafting
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: 8303.1: Core concepts and Freddie Mac policies for
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: reporting and drafting (12/17/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Definitions of terms specific to this chapter
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Freddie Mac’s rights
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Reporting and drafting
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Freddie Mac drafting timelines
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Availability of funds
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Draft dates
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Freddie Mac resources related to reporting and remittance
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Definitions of terms specific to this chapter
elements.label: text

elements.text: The following additional terms are used in this chapter. The terms are italicized here and
elements.label: text

elements.text: when they are used in the chapter.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Defined terms for Mortgage Servicing and reporting
elements.label: text

elements.text: A
elements.label: text

elements.text: Active
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: A current or delinquent Mortgage for which the Servicer reports and
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac drafts payments and payoffs.
elements.label: text

elements.text: B
elements.label: text

elements.text: Biweekly
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Mortgage that is originated and delivered to Freddie Mac with a
elements.label: text

elements.text: biweekly payment schedule in which the Borrower makes a principal and
elements.label: text

elements.text: interest payment every two weeks pursuant to either the terms of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note, a rider or a separate agreement.
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Mortgage in which the Servicer and Borrower enter a biweekly
elements.label: text

elements.text: payment plan in accordance with Section 8104.1(b) after the Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: has been sold to Freddie Mac is not considered a Biweekly Mortgage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: C
elements.label: text

elements.text: Compensating
elements.label: text

elements.text: Interest
elements.label: text

elements.text: For purposes of the requirements regarding Payoff Draft Date 18
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages, “Compensating Interest” is interest due to Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: calculated by subtracting Exception Interest from Monthly Interest. The
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer may not collect Compensating Interest from the Borrower.
elements.label: text

elements.text: E
elements.label: text

elements.text: Exception
elements.label: text

elements.text: Interest
elements.label: text

elements.text: Interest due to Freddie Mac calculated from the number of days from the
elements.label: text

elements.text: first calendar day of the month up to, but not including, the exception
elements.label: text

elements.text: date. The Exception Interest is calculated by multiplying the Ending
elements.label: text

elements.text: UPB of the prior Accounting Cycle, multiplied by the Accounting Net
elements.label: text

elements.text: Yield (ANY) divided by 365, multiplied by the number of days.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Exception (or
elements.label: text

elements.text: liquidation)
elements.label: text

elements.text: activity
elements.label: text

elements.text: Anything other than a regular monthly Principal and Interest Payment
elements.label: text

elements.text: and partial prepayment. It includes:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Payoff – matured
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Payoff – prepaid
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Payoff – repurchase
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Payoff – conversion (ARM converting to a fixed-rate Mortgage)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Payoff – short sale, charge-off or make-whole
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Third party foreclosure sale (conventional and FHA/VA)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Transfer to REO
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Deeds-in-lieu of foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ FHA/VA foreclosure conveyance
elements.label: text

elements.text: I
elements.label: text

elements.text: Inactive
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: An Active Mortgage becomes an Inactive Mortgage in the Accounting
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cycle in which it becomes 120 days delinquent (four payments past due).
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must change the Reporting Status to inactive either by
elements.label: text

elements.text: selecting “inactivation” in the Freddie Mac Loan Level Reporting tool
elements.label: text

elements.text: (see Exhibit 88, Servicing Tools) or reporting Loan Level Reporting
elements.label: text

elements.text: exception code 40 (Inactivation). If the Servicer does not inactivate a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage in the Accounting Cycle in which it becomes 120 days
elements.label: text

elements.text: delinquent, Freddie Mac will inactivate the Mortgage. Once a Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: is inactivated, no monthly principal and interest will be drafted unless the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage is partially or fully reinstated.

elements.label: text

elements.text: the Ending UPB of the prior Accounting Cycle, multiplied by the ANY
elements.label: text

elements.text: divided by 12.

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: The regular monthly Principal and Interest Payment due to Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: and any partial prepayment. This is also referred to as non-exception
elements.label: text

elements.text: activity.

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: due days” data field in Loan Selling Advisor®.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Payoff Date is on the first or second Business Day of the month
elements.label: text

elements.text: and the payoff is successfully reported on the first or second Business
elements.label: text

elements.text: Day of the month, Freddie Mac will draft funds on the 18th calendar day
elements.label: text

elements.text: of that month (i.e., in the month in which payoff was successfully
elements.label: text

elements.text: reported).
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Payoff Date is on or after the third Business Day of the month and
elements.label: text

elements.text: the payoff is successfully reported on or after the third Business Day of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the month, Freddie Mac will draft funds on the 18th calendar day of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: month following the Payoff Date (i.e., in the month after the month in
elements.label: text

elements.text: which the payoff was successfully reported.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: If the 18th calendar day is a non-Business Day, funds will be
elements.label: text

elements.text: drafted on the prior Business Day.

elements.text: The information listed in Exhibit 60, Loan-Level Reporting Data
elements.label: text

elements.text: Description, that the Servicer may report daily but must report to Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac at least monthly on or before the P&I Determination Date for all
elements.label: text

elements.text: Active and Inactive Mortgages the Servicer services for Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Freddie Mac’s rights
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac reserves the right to assess compensatory and other fees in accordance with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements of this chapter if the Servicer fails to comply with Freddie Mac’s reporting and
elements.label: text

elements.text: drafting requirements. The compensatory and other fees are set forth in Sections 8303.5.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Reporting and drafting
elements.label: text

elements.text: Reporting is the process of transmitting the Borrower’s payment status of all Freddie Mac’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages via one of the Freddie Mac-approved reporting media described in Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 8301.2(c). The Servicer’s transmissions must be usable, accurate and timely.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Based on Servicer monthly reporting, Freddie Mac will draft principal and interest and
elements.label: text

elements.text: payoff amounts that are due to Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must report a loan-level transaction on a monthly basis for all Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: serviced for Freddie Mac. Freddie Mac considers a loan-level transaction to be reported when
elements.label: text

elements.text: it has been transmitted by the Servicer without errors and received by Freddie Mac. If a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer does not report a loan-level transaction or does not clear all edits by the end of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Accounting Cycle, Freddie Mac will simulate a loan-level transaction and account for each
elements.label: text

elements.text: unreported Mortgage as delinquent based on the last reported DDLPI.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In addition, the Servicer is required to report liquidation activity to Freddie Mac as it occurs.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Monthly reporting activity
elements.label: text

elements.text: Daily reporting is encouraged but not required. The Servicer must report a loan-level
elements.label: text

elements.text: transaction for each Mortgage serviced for Freddie Mac once a month no later than 2:00
elements.label: text

elements.text: a.m. Eastern Time on the day following the P&I Determination Date regardless of
elements.label: text

elements.text: whether any activity has occurred on the Mortgage. For each Mortgage, Servicers must
elements.label: text

elements.text: report any revisions that occur after the P&I Determination Date by the end of the month
elements.label: text

elements.text: plus one Business Day. In addition, Servicers are required to monitor daily edit reports
elements.label: text

elements.text: provided by Freddie Mac and correct edits promptly, as provided by Section 8303.4(c).
elements.label: text

elements.text: Failure to comply with Freddie Mac’s reporting requirements will result in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: assessment of compensatory fees as set forth in Sections 8303.5(a) through 8303.5(i).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Remittance cycle and Automated Clearing House (ACH) draft
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Standard Remittance Cycle applies to all Mortgages serviced for Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will initiate an ACH draft to collect monthly Principal and Interest
elements.label: text

elements.text: Payments and payoffs directly from the Servicer’s designated Custodial Account in
elements.label: text

elements.text: accordance with the requirements of this section.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Monthly principal and interest will be drafted on the P&I Draft Date. The principal
elements.label: text

elements.text: drafted will be the actual principal collected and reported after 2:00 a.m. Eastern Time on
elements.label: text

elements.text: the day following the P&I Determination Date of the previous Accounting Cycle and the
elements.label: text

elements.text: actual principal collected and reported to Freddie Mac no later than 2:00 a.m. Eastern
elements.label: text

elements.text: Time on the day following the P&I Determination Date of the current Accounting Cycle.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Principal reported to Freddie Mac after 2:00 a.m. Eastern Time on the day following the
elements.label: text

elements.text: P&I Determination Date will be drafted on the P&I Draft Date of the next Accounting
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cycle. The interest drafted will be the amount of forecasted scheduled interest reported in
elements.label: text

elements.text: the prior Accounting Cycle.
elements.label: text

elements.text: To authorize ACH drafting of monthly Principal and Interest Payments and payoffs or to
elements.label: text

elements.text: make changes to ACH draft account instructions previously provided, the Servicer must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Have submitted a completed, executed and duly authorized Form 1132A,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Authorization for Automatic Transfer of Funds from Principal and Interest Custodial
elements.label: text

elements.text: Account Through the ACH
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Provide Freddie Mac with an updated Form 1132A for any change to the ACH
elements.label: text

elements.text: instructions no later than 15 Business Days before the last Business Day of the first
elements.label: text

elements.text: month in which Freddie Mac will initiate the first draft to set up or make certain
elements.label: text

elements.text: changes to the draft account
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Deliver Form 1132A to Freddie Mac as an Electronic Record (as defined in Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1401.1(b)), using a Portable Document Format (PDF) (or other Electronic Record
elements.label: text

elements.text: format commonly used in the mortgage industry), that has been completed and
elements.label: text

elements.text: contains the copy or representation of the pen and ink signature of the Servicer’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: Authorized Employee (such copy or representation of the Authorized Employee’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: signature shall be such Authorized Employee’s adopted Electronic Signature as
elements.label: text

elements.text: defined in Section 1401.1(b)) and either:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Attached to an e-mail and e-mailed to Freddie Mac at
elements.label: text

elements.text: CashCollections@FreddieMac.com; or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Uploaded through the Freddie Mac eBill system
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac and the Servicer agree that the delivery of Electronic Records with
elements.label: text

elements.text: Electronic Signatures are eligible Electronic Transactions (as defined in Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1401.1(b)) and are governed by the applicable provisions of Chapter 1401.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The employee authorized to execute Form 1132A on the Servicer’s behalf must be
elements.label: text

elements.text: designated as an “Authorized Employee” on the Servicer’s:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Form 988SF, Certificate of Incumbency for a Bank, Savings Bank, Savings and Loan
elements.label: text

elements.text: Association, Credit Union, Corporation or Limited Liability Company, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Form 989SF, Certificate of Incumbency for Limited Partnerships, as applicable (see
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 2201.1 for additional information regarding the Certificate of Incumbency
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will perform a call back to verify receipt of authorized Form 1132A ACH
elements.label: text

elements.text: instructions and will also process a non-dollar transaction using the information provided
elements.label: text

elements.text: on Form 1132A to verify the accuracy of the instructions.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The account the Servicer designates on Form 1132A as the ACH draft account for the
elements.label: text

elements.text: payment of monthly principal and interest and payoffs must be a Principal and Interest
elements.label: text

elements.text: Custodial Account or a Principal and Interest Disbursement Clearing Custodial Account.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer agrees to notify Freddie Mac immediately at
elements.label: text

elements.text: CashCollections@FreddieMac.com of any changes to the status of the Servicer’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: designated Custodial Account.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If funds in the Servicer’s designated Custodial Account are insufficient to cover the
elements.label: text

elements.text: amount due, or if Freddie Mac is unable to draft principal and interest payments or payoff
elements.label: text

elements.text: amounts from the Servicer’s Custodial Account for any reason, the Servicer may be
elements.label: text

elements.text: assessed a delayed draft compensatory fee in accordance with Section 8303.5(h).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii) Exception or liquidation activity
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must report repurchases and prepaid payoffs by the second Business Day
elements.label: text

elements.text: after the activity. Matured payoffs must be reported by the earlier of the maturity date or
elements.label: text

elements.text: within two Business Days after the date the Servicer receives the funds, as provided by
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 8303.3(a). The Servicer must report all other exception activity by the fifth
elements.label: text

elements.text: Business Day after the activity.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For Standard Payoff Remittances, if reported no later than the second Business Day after
elements.label: text

elements.text: the activity or, in the case of matured payoffs, two Business Days after funds are
elements.label: text

elements.text: received, Freddie Mac will draft payoff proceeds on the fifth Business Day after the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Payoff Date.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For Payoff Draft Date 18 Mortgage Payoff remittances:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If the Payoff Date is on the first or second Business Day of the month and the payoff
elements.label: text

elements.text: is successfully reported on the first or second Business Day of the month for matured
elements.label: text

elements.text: or prepaid payoffs, or repurchases, Freddie Mac will draft funds on the 18th calendar
elements.label: text

elements.text: day after the Payoff Date (i.e., in the month in which payoff was successfully
elements.label: text

elements.text: reported)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If the Payoff Date is on or after the third Business Day of the month and the payoff is
elements.label: text

elements.text: successfully reported on or after the third Business Day of the month for matured or
elements.label: text

elements.text: prepaid payoffs, or repurchases, Freddie Mac will draft funds on the 18th calendar day
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the month following the Payoff Date (i.e., in the month after the month in which
elements.label: text

elements.text: the payoff was successfully reported)
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer fails to report the payoff of a Mortgage that has reached maturity, Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac will simulate a payoff transaction for the Mortgage as of the first calendar day of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: month following the maturity date. The proceeds will post to the Draft Report and will be
elements.label: text

elements.text: drafted on the second Business Day after Freddie Mac simulates the matured payoff
elements.label: text

elements.text: transaction. However, if Freddie Mac elects not to draft a payoff transaction for a
elements.label: text

elements.text: matured Mortgage, Freddie Mac, in its sole discretion, may exercise its right to require
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Servicer to repurchase the Mortgage. The Servicer will be responsible for all accrued
elements.label: text

elements.text: interest through the date of payoff and the Servicer will incur a compensatory fee if the
elements.label: text

elements.text: funds are not available to Freddie Mac by the applicable Payoff Draft Date. In addition,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac reserves the right to assess a delayed draft noncompliance compensatory fee
elements.label: text

elements.text: (see Section 8303.5(h)) for all matured Mortgages for which the Servicer fails to report
elements.label: text

elements.text: timely.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer will receive an edit if the Servicer attempts to report a principal and interest
elements.label: text

elements.text: activity in the next Accounting Cycle following the month of maturity.
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: On a daily basis, Freddie Mac provides Servicers a Draft Report that provides a daily
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: Accounting Cycle. In addition, the report provides loan-level transaction details (e.g.,
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: the P&I Determination Date.
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Foreclosure, REO and escrow
elements.label: text

elements.text: The only funds the Servicer must remit by check or wire are REO (refer to Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9701.4(e)) proceeds and Escrow refunds for taxes or property insurance premiums in
elements.label: text

elements.text: connection with a foreclosure or REO. Principal and interest proceeds must be remitted
elements.label: text

elements.text: via the automated cash remittance system as described in Chapter 8302.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e) Availability of funds
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must ensure funds are available no later than 9:00 am (Eastern
elements.label: text

elements.text: Standard/Daylight Time) on the applicable draft date. If sufficient funds are not available on
elements.label: text

elements.text: the applicable draft date, Servicers may be assessed a draft delay noncompliance
elements.label: text

elements.text: compensatory fee as referenced in 8303.5(h).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (f) Draft dates
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must make all funds due to Freddie Mac available for Freddie Mac to draft by
elements.label: text

elements.text: the applicable draft date. If the applicable draft date is on a non-Business Day, the funds must
elements.label: text

elements.text: be available to Freddie Mac on the preceding Business Day. Refer to Section 8303.1(e) for
elements.label: text

elements.text: availability of remittances.

elements.text: (a) Definitions of terms specific to this chapter
elements.label: text

elements.text: The following additional terms are used in this chapter. The terms are italicized here and
elements.label: text

elements.text: when they are used in the chapter.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Defined terms for Mortgage Servicing and reporting
elements.label: text

elements.text: A
elements.label: text

elements.text: Active
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: A current or delinquent Mortgage for which the Servicer reports and
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac drafts payments and payoffs.
elements.label: text

elements.text: B
elements.label: text

elements.text: Biweekly
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Mortgage that is originated and delivered to Freddie Mac with a
elements.label: text

elements.text: biweekly payment schedule in which the Borrower makes a principal and
elements.label: text

elements.text: interest payment every two weeks pursuant to either the terms of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note, a rider or a separate agreement.
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Mortgage in which the Servicer and Borrower enter a biweekly
elements.label: text

elements.text: payment plan in accordance with Section 8104.1(b) after the Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: has been sold to Freddie Mac is not considered a Biweekly Mortgage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: C
elements.label: text

elements.text: Compensating
elements.label: text

elements.text: Interest
elements.label: text

elements.text: For purposes of the requirements regarding Payoff Draft Date 18
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages, “Compensating Interest” is interest due to Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: calculated by subtracting Exception Interest from Monthly Interest. The
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer may not collect Compensating Interest from the Borrower.
elements.label: text

elements.text: E
elements.label: text

elements.text: Exception
elements.label: text

elements.text: Interest
elements.label: text

elements.text: Interest due to Freddie Mac calculated from the number of days from the
elements.label: text

elements.text: first calendar day of the month up to, but not including, the exception
elements.label: text

elements.text: date. The Exception Interest is calculated by multiplying the Ending
elements.label: text

elements.text: UPB of the prior Accounting Cycle, multiplied by the Accounting Net
elements.label: text

elements.text: Yield (ANY) divided by 365, multiplied by the number of days.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Exception (or
elements.label: text

elements.text: liquidation)
elements.label: text

elements.text: activity
elements.label: text

elements.text: Anything other than a regular monthly Principal and Interest Payment
elements.label: text

elements.text: and partial prepayment. It includes:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Payoff – matured
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Payoff – prepaid
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Payoff – repurchase
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Payoff – conversion (ARM converting to a fixed-rate Mortgage)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Payoff – short sale, charge-off or make-whole
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Third party foreclosure sale (conventional and FHA/VA)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Transfer to REO
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Deeds-in-lieu of foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ FHA/VA foreclosure conveyance
elements.label: text

elements.text: I
elements.label: text

elements.text: Inactive
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: An Active Mortgage becomes an Inactive Mortgage in the Accounting
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cycle in which it becomes 120 days delinquent (four payments past due).
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must change the Reporting Status to inactive either by
elements.label: text

elements.text: selecting “inactivation” in the Freddie Mac Loan Level Reporting tool
elements.label: text

elements.text: (see Exhibit 88, Servicing Tools) or reporting Loan Level Reporting
elements.label: text

elements.text: exception code 40 (Inactivation). If the Servicer does not inactivate a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage in the Accounting Cycle in which it becomes 120 days
elements.label: text

elements.text: delinquent, Freddie Mac will inactivate the Mortgage. Once a Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: is inactivated, no monthly principal and interest will be drafted unless the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage is partially or fully reinstated.
elements.label: text

elements.text: M
elements.label: text

elements.text: Monthly
elements.label: text

elements.text: Interest
elements.label: text

elements.text: Interest due to Freddie Mac calculated for the month of payoff (i.e., the
elements.label: text

elements.text: exception date). The monthly interest is calculated by multiplying
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Ending UPB of the prior Accounting Cycle, multiplied by the ANY
elements.label: text

elements.text: divided by 12.
elements.label: text

elements.text: P
elements.label: text

elements.text: Principal and
elements.label: text

elements.text: Interest
elements.label: text

elements.text: Activity
elements.label: text

elements.text: The regular monthly Principal and Interest Payment due to Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: and any partial prepayment. This is also referred to as non-exception
elements.label: text

elements.text: activity.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Payoff Draft
elements.label: text

elements.text: Date 18
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages delivered that reflect an “18” in the “prepayment remittance
elements.label: text

elements.text: due days” data field in Loan Selling Advisor®.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Payoff Date is on the first or second Business Day of the month
elements.label: text

elements.text: and the payoff is successfully reported on the first or second Business
elements.label: text

elements.text: Day of the month, Freddie Mac will draft funds on the 18th calendar day
elements.label: text

elements.text: of that month (i.e., in the month in which payoff was successfully
elements.label: text

elements.text: reported).
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Payoff Date is on or after the third Business Day of the month and
elements.label: text

elements.text: the payoff is successfully reported on or after the third Business Day of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the month, Freddie Mac will draft funds on the 18th calendar day of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: month following the Payoff Date (i.e., in the month after the month in
elements.label: text

elements.text: which the payoff was successfully reported.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: If the 18th calendar day is a non-Business Day, funds will be
elements.label: text

elements.text: drafted on the prior Business Day.
elements.label: text

elements.text: R
elements.label: text

elements.text: Reporting
elements.label: text

elements.text: Status
elements.label: text

elements.text: The information listed in Exhibit 60, Loan-Level Reporting Data
elements.label: text

elements.text: Description, that the Servicer may report daily but must report to Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac at least monthly on or before the P&I Determination Date for all
elements.label: text

elements.text: Active and Inactive Mortgages the Servicer services for Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Freddie Mac’s rights
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac reserves the right to assess compensatory and other fees in accordance with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements of this chapter if the Servicer fails to comply with Freddie Mac’s reporting and
elements.label: text

elements.text: drafting requirements. The compensatory and other fees are set forth in Sections 8303.5.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Reporting and drafting
elements.label: text

elements.text: Reporting is the process of transmitting the Borrower’s payment status of all Freddie Mac’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages via one of the Freddie Mac-approved reporting media described in Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 8301.2(c). The Servicer’s transmissions must be usable, accurate and timely.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Based on Servicer monthly reporting, Freddie Mac will draft principal and interest and
elements.label: text

elements.text: payoff amounts that are due to Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must report a loan-level transaction on a monthly basis for all Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: serviced for Freddie Mac. Freddie Mac considers a loan-level transaction to be reported when
elements.label: text

elements.text: it has been transmitted by the Servicer without errors and received by Freddie Mac. If a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer does not report a loan-level transaction or does not clear all edits by the end of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Accounting Cycle, Freddie Mac will simulate a loan-level transaction and account for each
elements.label: text

elements.text: unreported Mortgage as delinquent based on the last reported DDLPI.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In addition, the Servicer is required to report liquidation activity to Freddie Mac as it occurs.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Monthly reporting activity
elements.label: text

elements.text: Daily reporting is encouraged but not required. The Servicer must report a loan-level
elements.label: text

elements.text: transaction for each Mortgage serviced for Freddie Mac once a month no later than 2:00
elements.label: text

elements.text: a.m. Eastern Time on the day following the P&I Determination Date regardless of
elements.label: text

elements.text: whether any activity has occurred on the Mortgage. For each Mortgage, Servicers must
elements.label: text

elements.text: report any revisions that occur after the P&I Determination Date by the end of the month
elements.label: text

elements.text: plus one Business Day. In addition, Servicers are required to monitor daily edit reports
elements.label: text

elements.text: provided by Freddie Mac and correct edits promptly, as provided by Section 8303.4(c).
elements.label: text

elements.text: Failure to comply with Freddie Mac’s reporting requirements will result in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: assessment of compensatory fees as set forth in Sections 8303.5(a) through 8303.5(i).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Remittance cycle and Automated Clearing House (ACH) draft
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Standard Remittance Cycle applies to all Mortgages serviced for Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will initiate an ACH draft to collect monthly Principal and Interest
elements.label: text

elements.text: Payments and payoffs directly from the Servicer’s designated Custodial Account in
elements.label: text

elements.text: accordance with the requirements of this section.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Monthly principal and interest will be drafted on the P&I Draft Date. The principal
elements.label: text

elements.text: drafted will be the actual principal collected and reported after 2:00 a.m. Eastern Time on
elements.label: text

elements.text: the day following the P&I Determination Date of the previous Accounting Cycle and the
elements.label: text

elements.text: actual principal collected and reported to Freddie Mac no later than 2:00 a.m. Eastern
elements.label: text

elements.text: Time on the day following the P&I Determination Date of the current Accounting Cycle.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Principal reported to Freddie Mac after 2:00 a.m. Eastern Time on the day following the
elements.label: text

elements.text: P&I Determination Date will be drafted on the P&I Draft Date of the next Accounting
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cycle. The interest drafted will be the amount of forecasted scheduled interest reported in
elements.label: text

elements.text: the prior Accounting Cycle.
elements.label: text

elements.text: To authorize ACH drafting of monthly Principal and Interest Payments and payoffs or to
elements.label: text

elements.text: make changes to ACH draft account instructions previously provided, the Servicer must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Have submitted a completed, executed and duly authorized Form 1132A,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Authorization for Automatic Transfer of Funds from Principal and Interest Custodial
elements.label: text

elements.text: Account Through the ACH
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Provide Freddie Mac with an updated Form 1132A for any change to the ACH
elements.label: text

elements.text: instructions no later than 15 Business Days before the last Business Day of the first
elements.label: text

elements.text: month in which Freddie Mac will initiate the first draft to set up or make certain
elements.label: text

elements.text: changes to the draft account
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Deliver Form 1132A to Freddie Mac as an Electronic Record (as defined in Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1401.1(b)), using a Portable Document Format (PDF) (or other Electronic Record
elements.label: text

elements.text: format commonly used in the mortgage industry), that has been completed and
elements.label: text

elements.text: contains the copy or representation of the pen and ink signature of the Servicer’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: Authorized Employee (such copy or representation of the Authorized Employee’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: signature shall be such Authorized Employee’s adopted Electronic Signature as
elements.label: text

elements.text: defined in Section 1401.1(b)) and either:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Attached to an e-mail and e-mailed to Freddie Mac at
elements.label: text

elements.text: CashCollections@FreddieMac.com; or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Uploaded through the Freddie Mac eBilling system
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac and the Servicer agree that the delivery of Electronic Records with
elements.label: text

elements.text: Electronic Signatures are eligible Electronic Transactions (as defined in Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1401.1(b)) and are governed by the applicable provisions of Chapter 1401.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The employee authorized to execute Form 1132A on the Servicer’s behalf must be
elements.label: text

elements.text: designated as an “Authorized Employee” on the Servicer’s:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Form 988SF, Certificate of Incumbency for a Bank, Savings Bank, Savings and Loan
elements.label: text

elements.text: Association, Credit Union, Corporation or Limited Liability Company, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Form 989SF, Certificate of Incumbency for Limited Partnerships, as applicable (see
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 2201.1 for additional information regarding the Certificate of Incumbency
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will perform a call back to verify receipt of authorized Form 1132A ACH
elements.label: text

elements.text: instructions and will also process a non-dollar transaction using the information provided
elements.label: text

elements.text: on Form 1132A to verify the accuracy of the instructions.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The account the Servicer designates on Form 1132A as the ACH draft account for the
elements.label: text

elements.text: payment of monthly principal and interest and payoffs must be a Principal and Interest
elements.label: text

elements.text: Custodial Account or a Principal and Interest Disbursement Clearing Custodial Account.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer agrees to notify Freddie Mac immediately at
elements.label: text

elements.text: CashCollections@FreddieMac.com of any changes to the status of the Servicer’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: designated Custodial Account.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If funds in the Servicer’s designated Custodial Account are insufficient to cover the
elements.label: text

elements.text: amount due, or if Freddie Mac is unable to draft principal and interest payments or payoff
elements.label: text

elements.text: amounts from the Servicer’s Custodial Account for any reason, the Servicer may be
elements.label: text

elements.text: assessed a delayed draft compensatory fee in accordance with Section 8303.5(h).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii) Exception or liquidation activity
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must report repurchases and prepaid payoffs by the second Business Day
elements.label: text

elements.text: after the activity. Matured payoffs must be reported by the earlier of the maturity date or
elements.label: text

elements.text: within two Business Days after the date the Servicer receives the funds, as provided by
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 8303.3(a). The Servicer must report all other exception activity by the fifth
elements.label: text

elements.text: Business Day after the activity.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For Standard Payoff Remittances, if reported no later than the second Business Day after
elements.label: text

elements.text: the activity or, in the case of matured payoffs, two Business Days after funds are
elements.label: text

elements.text: received, Freddie Mac will draft payoff proceeds on the fifth Business Day after the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Payoff Date.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For Payoff Draft Date 18 Mortgage Payoff remittances:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If the Payoff Date is on the first or second Business Day of the month and the payoff
elements.label: text

elements.text: is successfully reported on the first or second Business Day of the month for matured
elements.label: text

elements.text: or prepaid payoffs, or repurchases, Freddie Mac will draft funds on the 18th calendar
elements.label: text

elements.text: day after the Payoff Date (i.e., in the month in which payoff was successfully
elements.label: text

elements.text: reported)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If the Payoff Date is on or after the third Business Day of the month and the payoff is
elements.label: text

elements.text: successfully reported on or after the third Business Day of the month for matured or
elements.label: text

elements.text: prepaid payoffs, or repurchases, Freddie Mac will draft funds on the 18th calendar day
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the month following the Payoff Date (i.e., in the month after the month in which
elements.label: text

elements.text: the payoff was successfully reported)
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer fails to report the payoff of a Mortgage that has reached maturity, Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac will simulate a payoff transaction for the Mortgage as of the first calendar day of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: month following the maturity date. The proceeds will post to the Draft Report and will be
elements.label: text

elements.text: drafted on the second Business Day after Freddie Mac simulates the matured payoff
elements.label: text

elements.text: transaction. However, if Freddie Mac elects not to draft a payoff transaction for a
elements.label: text

elements.text: matured Mortgage, Freddie Mac, in its sole discretion, may exercise its right to require
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Servicer to repurchase the Mortgage. The Servicer will be responsible for all accrued
elements.label: text

elements.text: interest through the date of payoff and the Servicer will incur a compensatory fee if the
elements.label: text

elements.text: funds are not available to Freddie Mac by the applicable Payoff Draft Date. In addition,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac reserves the right to assess a delayed draft noncompliance compensatory fee
elements.label: text

elements.text: (see Section 8303.5(h)) for all matured Mortgages for which the Servicer fails to report
elements.label: text

elements.text: timely.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer will receive an edit if the Servicer attempts to report a principal and interest
elements.label: text

elements.text: activity in the next Accounting Cycle following the month of maturity.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Monthly drafting
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will draft, via an ACH transaction, monthly principal and interest and
elements.label: text

elements.text: payoffs directly from the Servicer’s designated Custodial Account.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Monthly principal and interest will be drafted on the P&I Draft Date (the second Business
elements.label: text

elements.text: Day after the P&I Determination Date) and will include:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Current Accounting Cycle interest due (reported in the prior Accounting Cycle)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Actual principal collected and reported to Freddie Mac after 2:00 a.m. Eastern Time
elements.label: text

elements.text: on the day following the P&I Determination Date of the prior Accounting Cycle
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Actual principal collected during the current Accounting Cycle and reported to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac no later than 2:00 a.m. Eastern Time on the day following the P&I
elements.label: text

elements.text: Determination Date of the current Accounting Cycle
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Interest due on reinstated Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: On a daily basis, Freddie Mac provides Servicers a Draft Report that provides a daily
elements.label: text

elements.text: cumulative view of the amounts, including adjustments, that will be drafted during the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Accounting Cycle. In addition, the report provides loan-level transaction details (e.g.,
elements.label: text

elements.text: principal and interest, liquidation amounts and adjustments). A preliminary Draft Report
elements.label: text

elements.text: will be available daily in the Freddie Mac Cash Manager tool (see Exhibit 88) and
elements.label: text

elements.text: updated based on daily reporting. A final version will be provided on the morning after
elements.label: text

elements.text: the P&I Determination Date.
elements.label: text

elements.text: To authorize ACH drafting of monthly principal and interest and payoffs, the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: must complete, execute and submit to Freddie Mac Form 1132A. See Section 8303.1(c)
elements.label: text

elements.text: for additional information about Form 1132A delivery requirements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: See Sections 8303.3(a) through 8303.3(f) for drafting requirements for payoffs and
elements.label: text

elements.text: liquidations.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Foreclosure, REO and escrow
elements.label: text

elements.text: The only funds the Servicer must remit by check or wire are REO (refer to Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9701.4(e)) proceeds and Escrow refunds for taxes or property insurance premiums in
elements.label: text

elements.text: connection with a foreclosure or REO. Principal and interest proceeds must be remitted
elements.label: text

elements.text: via the automated cash remittance system as described in Chapter 8302.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e) Availability of funds
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must ensure funds are available no later than 9:00 am (Eastern
elements.label: text

elements.text: Standard/Daylight Time) on the applicable draft date. If sufficient funds are not available on
elements.label: text

elements.text: the applicable draft date, Servicers may be assessed a draft delay noncompliance
elements.label: text

elements.text: compensatory fee as referenced in 8303.5(h).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (f) Draft dates
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must make all funds due to Freddie Mac available for Freddie Mac to draft by
elements.label: text

elements.text: the applicable draft date. If the applicable draft date is on a non-Business Day, the funds must
elements.label: text

elements.text: be available to Freddie Mac on the preceding Business Day. Refer to Section 8303.1(e) for
elements.label: text

elements.text: availability of remittances.

elements.text: Monthly loan-level reporting requirements for all Mortgages with a partial

elements.text: Data field Description
elements.label: text

elements.text: Current UPB For all Mortgages, report the UPB of the Mortgage as
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the end of the Accounting Cycle.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For Mortgages with partial principal forbearance,
elements.label: text

elements.text: the reported UPB must equal the sum of the interest￾bearing UPB (the amortizing principal balance of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage or the “interest-bearing UPB”) and the
elements.label: text

elements.text: principal forbearance balance (“deferred UPB”), as of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the end of the Accounting Cycle.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Interest-bearing UPB For Mortgages with partial principal forbearance,
elements.label: text

elements.text: report the amount of the interest-bearing UPB (the
elements.label: text

elements.text: amortizing principal balance of the Mortgage) as of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the end of the Accounting Cycle. (Note: Monthly
elements.label: text

elements.text: “Interest Due Freddie Mac” must be calculated and
elements.label: text

elements.text: reported based on the interest-bearing UPB only.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Deferred UPB For Mortgages with partial principal forbearance,
elements.label: text

elements.text: report the amount of deferred UPB as of the end of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Accounting Cycle. (Note: The deferred UPB is non￾interest-bearing and non-amortizing and will be due in
elements.label: text

elements.text: the form of a balloon payment upon the earlier of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: transfer of all or a portion of the property, the payoff
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the interest-bearing UPB or the new maturity date
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the modified Mortgage.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Principal Due Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: For all Mortgages, report Freddie Mac’s share of
elements.label: text

elements.text: principal payments, including prepayments of
elements.label: text

elements.text: principal (i.e., curtailments) applied to the interest￾bearing UPB of the Mortgage during the Accounting
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cycle.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Deferred Principal
elements.label: text

elements.text: Curtailment Amount
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: report the amount of any principal curtailment applied

elements.text: (ii) Exception reporting and remitting for a payoff of a Mortgage with partial principal
elements.label: text

elements.text: forbearance
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers must comply with the applicable exception reporting requirements set forth in
elements.label: text

elements.text: this chapter except that, when reporting the payoff of a Mortgage with a partial principal
elements.label: text

elements.text: forbearance, Servicers must also comply with the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Current UPB, Interest-bearing UPB and Deferred UPB fields must be reported as
elements.label: text

elements.text: zero
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The amount of deferred UPB as reported in the previous Accounting Cycle must be
elements.label: text

elements.text: reported in the Deferred Principal Curtailment Amount field
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The amount of the current UPB (i.e., combined interest-bearing UPB and deferred
elements.label: text

elements.text: UPB) as reported in the previous Accounting Cycle, plus or minus the Exception
elements.label: text

elements.text: Interest, will be drafted with the Servicer’s exception remittance. Exception Interest
elements.label: text

elements.text: must be based on the interest-bearing UPB only.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers must calculate payoff proceeds in accordance with the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Interest-Bearing UPB + Deferred UPB +/- Exception Interest (calculated based on the
elements.label: text

elements.text: interest-bearing UPB) = Proceeds due Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii) Application of partial prepayments of principal
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the partial prepayment of principal (“principal curtailment”) is less than the interest￾bearing UPB, it must be applied to the interest-bearing UPB. If the principal curtailment
elements.label: text

elements.text: is equal to or greater than the interest-bearing UPB, then the principal curtailment must
elements.label: text

elements.text: first be applied to the deferred UPB and any remaining curtailment to the interest-bearing
elements.label: text

elements.text: UPB.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers must report the amount of any curtailment applied to the deferred UPB during
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Accounting Cycle in the Deferred Principal Curtailment Amount data field.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iv) Monthly statements
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac recommends the Servicer include the amount of the deferred UPB and the
elements.label: text

elements.text: combined interest-bearing and deferred UPBs on the Borrower’s monthly statement.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (v) Deferred UPB outstanding
elements.label: text

elements.text: For Mortgages with a deferred UPB (also referred to as non-interest bearing UPB), in
elements.label: text

elements.text: accordance with the modification agreement, Payment Deferral agreement and/or Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 8303.2(a), as applicable, the deferred UPB is due upon the earliest of the (i) transfer or
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: the Mortgage maturity date (as modified) and must be reported in accordance with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements in Section 8203.2(a)(ii).
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer does not receive the full payoff, including the deferred UPB, or the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower is not in active review for a workout by the end of the Accounting Cycle of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: month of the transfer or sale of all or a portion of the property, payoff of the interest￾bearing UPB, or the Mortgage maturity date(as modified), the Servicer must proceed with
elements.label: text

elements.text: collections and default activities, as applicable, in compliance with the requirements set
elements.label: text

elements.text: forth in Chapters 9101 and 9102 and applicable law.

elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Payoff – matured or prepaid reporting and drafting requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: A payoff is the repayment of a Mortgage due to full amortization or prepayment-in-full. The
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer must report the payoff as of the Payoff Date or the date the Servicer receives the
elements.label: text

elements.text: funds (as applicable) and may not alter it for any reason.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must complete the following steps to report a payoff of a Mortgage:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. To report a prepayment-in-full, report the payoff by the second Business Day after the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Payoff Date.
elements.label: text

elements.text: To report the payoff of a matured Mortgage, report the payoff by the earlier of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: maturity date or within two Business Days of the date the Servicer receives the funds. For
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages registered on the MERS® System, the Servicer must, by the second Business
elements.label: text

elements.text: Day after the Payoff Date, update the MERS System to a loan status of “Paid in full” for
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages reported to Freddie Mac as matured or prepaid.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Do not report on this Mortgage in future Accounting Cycles
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will draft the payoff amount directly from the Servicer’s designated Custodial
elements.label: text

elements.text: Account as follows.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Principal
elements.label: text

elements.text: For standard payoff remittances:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ For a payoff of a matured Mortgage or a prepayment-in-full that is successfully
elements.label: text

elements.text: reported within two Business Days of the Exception Date, Freddie Mac will draft the
elements.label: text

elements.text: UPB due as reported in the previous Accounting Cycle on the fifth Business Day after
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Payoff Date
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ For a payoff of a matured Mortgage, or a prepayment-in-full that is successfully
elements.label: text

elements.text: reported more than two Business Days after the exception date (which is considered
elements.label: text

elements.text: late), Freddie Mac will draft the UPB due as reported in the previous Accounting
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cycle on the second Business Day after the reported payoff is successfully processed
elements.label: text
