elements.text: (b) Third-party foreclosure sale reporting and drafting requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: A third-party foreclosure sale is a sale of the property where title to the property is not
elements.label: text

elements.text: conveyed to Freddie Mac. The Servicer must comply with the requirements of Chapter 9301
elements.label: text

elements.text: regarding foreclosures and Section 8106.2(b) regarding the submission on Freddie Mac’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: behalf of Internal Revenue Service (IRS) Form 1099-A, Acquisition or Abandonment of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Secured Property.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In accordance with Section 9301.9(a), the Servicer must notify Freddie Mac of the third￾party foreclosure sale no later than one Business Day immediately following the date of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure sale.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must complete the following steps when the Servicer receives the entire amount
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the third-party foreclosure sale proceeds:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Report the transaction ID of “Foreclosure” and the sale result of “third-party sale” in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Loan Level Reporting tool (see Exhibit 88, Servicing Tools) no later than the second
elements.label: text

elements.text: Business Day after the Servicer receives the proceeds. When reporting, the Servicer must
elements.label: text

elements.text: ensure that the:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Foreclosure sale date is the date that the foreclosure sale occurred
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Ending gross UPB is zero
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Principal due field is completed with the gross UPB as of the last reporting
elements.label: text

elements.text: Accounting Cycle before the Mortgage was inactivated
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ DDLPI is the date of the last fully paid monthly installment
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Freddie Mac will draft directly from the Servicer’s designated Custodial Account as
elements.label: text

elements.text: follows:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Freddie Mac will draft the prior Accounting Cycle Ending UPB on the fifth Business
elements.label: text

elements.text: Day after the reported transaction is successfully processed in Loan Level Reporting
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Freddie Mac will draft the exception and delinquent interest on the fifth Business Day
elements.label: text

elements.text: after the reported transaction is successfully processed in Loan Level Reporting
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. Charge-off adjustments for third-party foreclosure sales that settle in Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: systems on or before the Payoff Determination Date will be reflected in the Draft Report
elements.label: text

elements.text: on the same date as the payoff draft. For transactions that settle in Freddie Mac systems
elements.label: text

elements.text: after the Payoff Determination Date, charge-off adjustments will be reflected in the Draft
elements.label: text

elements.text: Report on the second Business Day after the foreclosure sale settles in Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: systems. The Servicer must review the Draft Report with respect to charge-offs and
elements.label: text

elements.text: report any discrepancies to Freddie Mac via the Freddie Mac Servicing Data Corrections
elements.label: text

elements.text: tool (see Exhibit 88) within the same Accounting Cycle in which Freddie Mac posts the
elements.label: text

elements.text: amount to the Draft Report. Servicers may access the Draft Report through the Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac Cash Manager tool (see Exhibit 88).
elements.label: text

elements.text: When reporting a discrepancy, Servicers must input the calculation used to determine the
elements.label: text

elements.text: variance and upload any documentation to support the request in the Servicing Data
elements.label: text

elements.text: Corrections tool.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Discrepancies that are after the Accounting Cycle in which the initial adjustment is
elements.label: text

elements.text: posted to the Draft Report closes will be processed at Freddie Mac’s discretion and may
elements.label: text

elements.text: be subject to a contract noncompliance and contract change compensatory fee (see
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 8303.5(i)). If the post-settlement correction request is denied, the Servicer may
elements.label: text

elements.text: be liable for any additional losses.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. Do not report on this Mortgage in future cycles
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Short sale reporting and drafting requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: A short sale is the sale of a Mortgaged Premises for which the Servicer has received Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac’s approval to sell for less than the total amount necessary to satisfy the Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: obligation. The Servicer must comply with the requirements of Sections 9208.1(a) through
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9208.3(a) regarding a short sale and charging off a deficiency and Section 8106.2(c)
elements.label: text

elements.text: regarding the submission on Freddie Mac’s behalf of IRS Form 1099-C.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must complete the following steps to report when the Servicer receives the
elements.label: text

elements.text: funds for a short sale:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Report the short sale as “Short Sale/Charge-off/Make-whole” in the Loan Level
elements.label: text

elements.text: Reporting tool (see Exhibit 88) by the second Business Day after the Servicer receives
elements.label: text

elements.text: the settlement proceeds
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Freddie Mac will draft the Ending UPB and delinquent and Exception Interest on the fifth
elements.label: text

elements.text: Business Day after the exception date. If the short sale settles in Freddie Mac systems on
elements.label: text

elements.text: or before the Payoff Draft Determination Date, charge-off adjustments will be reflected
elements.label: text

elements.text: in the Draft Report on the same day as the payoff draft. If the short sale settles in Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac systems after the Payoff Draft Determination Date, the charge-off adjustment will be
elements.label: text

elements.text: reflected in the Draft Report on the second Business Day after the short sale settles in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac systems. See Section 8303.3(d) for additional requirements related to make￾whole preforeclosure sales.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. The Servicer must review the Draft Report with respect to charge-offs and report any
elements.label: text

elements.text: discrepancies to Freddie Mac via the Freddie Mac Servicing Data Corrections tool (see
elements.label: text

elements.text: Exhibit 88) within 30 calendar days following Freddie Mac’s posting of the amount to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Draft Report. Servicers may access the Draft Report through the Freddie Mac Cash
elements.label: text

elements.text: Manager tool.
elements.label: text

elements.text: When reporting a discrepancy, Servicers must input the calculation used to determine the
elements.label: text

elements.text: variance and upload any documentation to support the request in the Servicing Data
elements.label: text

elements.text: Corrections tool.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Discrepancies submitted more than 60 calendar days after the initial adjustment is posted
elements.label: text

elements.text: to the Draft Report will be processed at Freddie Mac’s discretion and may be subject to a
elements.label: text

elements.text: contract noncompliance and contract change compensatory fee (see Section 8303.5(i)). If
elements.label: text

elements.text: the post-settlement correction request is denied, the Servicer may be liable for any
elements.label: text

elements.text: additional losses. Additionally, Freddie Mac may recover any workout incentives that
elements.label: text

elements.text: were paid.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. Do not report on this Mortgage in future Accounting Cycles
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Make-whole preforeclosure sale reporting and drafting requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: A make-whole preforeclosure sale is the sale of a Mortgaged Premises for which the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: has received Freddie Mac’s approval to sell the property for less than the total amount
elements.label: text

elements.text: necessary to satisfy the Mortgage obligation; however, there is a mortgage insurance claim
elements.label: text

elements.text: payment or a Borrower contribution that satisfies the deficiency. The Servicer must comply
elements.label: text

elements.text: with the requirements of Sections 9208.1(c)(i)(B) and 9208.2(a)(ii) regarding a make-whole
elements.label: text

elements.text: preforeclosure sale.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must complete the following steps to report and remit when the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: receives the proceeds from a make-whole preforeclosure sale:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Report the Mortgage as “Short Sale/Charge-off/Make-whole” in the Loan Level
elements.label: text

elements.text: Reporting tool (see Exhibit 88) by the second Business Day after the Servicer receives
elements.label: text

elements.text: the settlement proceeds
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Not report on this Mortgage in future Accounting Cycles
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will draft the Ending UPB plus delinquent and Exception Interest on the
elements.label: text

elements.text: fifth Business Day after the exception date. If the make-whole preforeclosure sale settles
elements.label: text

elements.text: in Freddie Mac systems on or before the Payoff Draft Determination Date, charge-off
elements.label: text

elements.text: adjustments will be reflected in the Draft Report on the same day as the payoff draft. If
elements.label: text

elements.text: the make-whole preforeclosure settles in Freddie Mac systems after the Payoff Draft
elements.label: text

elements.text: Determination Date, the charge-off adjustment will be reflected in the Draft Report on the
elements.label: text

elements.text: second Business Day after the make-whole preforeclosure sale settles in Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: systems.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e) Reporting a charge-off to Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac’s approval of a charge-off request ceases collection efforts on a delinquent
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage when the debt is deemed to be uncollectible or that a foreclosure should not be
elements.label: text

elements.text: completed. In most cases, a charge-off will be accompanied by a lien release and cancelation
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the Note (see Section 9210.1(c)). The Servicer must comply with the requirements of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Sections 9210.1 and 9210.2 regarding a charge-off.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must complete the following steps to report a charge-off:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Report the charge-off as “Short Sale/Charge-off/Make-whole” in the Loan Level
elements.label: text

elements.text: Reporting tool (see Exhibit 88) within two Business Days of the workout approval date
elements.label: text

elements.text: reflected in Resolve® or following receipt of funds when a short-payoff has been
elements.label: text

elements.text: approved by Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Freddie Mac will draft the Ending UPB and delinquent and Exception Interest on the fifth
elements.label: text

elements.text: Business Day after the exception date. If the charge-off settles in Freddie Mac systems on
elements.label: text

elements.text: or before the Payoff Draft Determination Date, charge-off adjustments will be reflected
elements.label: text

elements.text: in the Draft Report on the same day as the payoff draft. If the charge-off settles in Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac systems after the Payoff Draft Determination Date, the charge-off adjustment will be
elements.label: text

elements.text: reflected in the Draft Report on the second Business Day after the charge-off settles in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac systems.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. The Servicer must review the Draft Report with respect to charge-offs and report any
elements.label: text

elements.text: discrepancies to Freddie Mac via the Freddie Mac Servicing Data Corrections tool (see
elements.label: text

elements.text: Exhibit 88) within 30 calendar days following Freddie Mac’s posting of the amount to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Draft Report. Servicers may access the Draft Report through the Freddie Mac Cash
elements.label: text

elements.text: Manager tool. When reporting a discrepancy, Servicers must input the calculation used to
elements.label: text

elements.text: determine the variance and upload any documentation to support the request in Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Data Corrections.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Discrepancies submitted more than 60 calendar days after the initial adjustment is posted
elements.label: text

elements.text: to the Draft Report will be processed at Freddie Mac’s discretion and may be subject to a
elements.label: text

elements.text: contract noncompliance and contract change compensatory fee (see Section 8303.5(i)). If
elements.label: text

elements.text: the postsettlement correction request is denied, the Servicer may be liable for any
elements.label: text

elements.text: additional losses.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. Do not report on this Mortgage in future Accounting Cycles
elements.label: text

elements.text: (g) Loan-level transaction reporting
elements.label: text

elements.text: The loan-level transaction is what Freddie Mac calls the monthly reporting of each Active
elements.label: text

elements.text: and Inactive Mortgage the Servicer services for Freddie Mac, including reporting monthly
elements.label: text

elements.text: net yield interest, principal reductions and principal increases due to Negative Amortization.

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: For Biweekly Mortgages, Servicers must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Report the biweekly contractual principal and interest payment as stated on the Note. If
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Servicer reports more than one payment in an Accounting Cycle, then each loan-level
elements.label: text

elements.text: transaction must reflect the cumulative principal received and forecasted scheduled
elements.label: text

elements.text: interest for that Accounting Cycle.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Report the DDLPI for each biweekly installment. However, if the Due Date falls on the
elements.label: text

elements.text: first day of the month, Servicers must report that DDLPI as the second day of the month.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: This does not apply to Mortgages where the Servicer and Borrower agree to a biweekly
elements.label: text

elements.text: payment plan in accordance with Section 8104.1(b) after the Mortgage has been sold to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Requirements for reporting and drafting of monthly Principal and Interest Activity and
elements.label: text

elements.text: certain exception activities are detailed in Section 8303.2(d)(i).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (h) Newly funded Mortgage reporting and drafting requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: A newly funded Mortgage is a Mortgage sold to Freddie Mac during the current Accounting
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cycle. The Funding Date and the Mortgage balance at the end of the Accounting Cycle
elements.label: text

elements.text: determine when the Servicer must report a newly funded Mortgage. The Servicer must
elements.label: text

elements.text: comply with the requirements of Section 8302.3(f) regarding the initial reporting and drafting
elements.label: text

elements.text: of a newly funded Mortgage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: To report a newly funded Mortgage, the Servicer must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Report prepayments or principal curtailments, if applicable, on a newly funded Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: to Freddie Mac in the current Accounting Cycle. The Servicer may report forecasted
elements.label: text

elements.text: scheduled interest by the end of the current Accounting Cycle
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. If prepayments or principal curtailments are reported prior to the P&I Determination
elements.label: text

elements.text: Date, Freddie Mac will draft principal due to Freddie Mac on the P&I Draft Date. If
elements.label: text

elements.text: prepayments or principal curtailments are received after the P&I Determination Date,
elements.label: text

elements.text: they must be reported by the end of the Accounting Cycle and principal will be drafted on
elements.label: text

elements.text: the P&I Draft Date of the next Accounting Cycle
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. Continue to report monthly. See Section 8303.1(c)(i) for monthly reporting requirements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Reporting inactivation of a Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: Inactivation is the process to suspend remitting funds to Freddie Mac for a Mortgage that is
elements.label: text

elements.text: 120 days delinquent. The Servicer must change the Reporting Status to inactive either by
elements.label: text

elements.text: selecting “inactivation” in the Freddie Mac Loan Level Reporting tool (see Exhibit 88) or
elements.label: text

elements.text: reporting Loan Level Reporting exception code 40 (Inactivation). If the Servicer does not
elements.label: text

elements.text: inactivate a Mortgage in the Accounting Cycle in which the Mortgage becomes 120 days
elements.label: text

elements.text: delinquent, Freddie Mac will inactivate the Mortgage. Once a Mortgage is inactivated, no
elements.label: text

elements.text: monthly principal and interest will be drafted unless the Mortgage is partially or fully
elements.label: text

elements.text: reinstated.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: This does not apply to Biweekly Mortgages that were originated and delivered with
elements.label: text

elements.text: biweekly payment schedules that become 120 days delinquent. Servicers should report $0
elements.label: text

elements.text: principal and $0 scheduled interest and not advance the DDLPI until at least one full
elements.label: text

elements.text: biweekly payment is collected from the Borrower. If a Servicer attempts to inactivate a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Biweekly Mortgage that becomes 120 days delinquent by doing either of the following, it
elements.label: text

elements.text: will receive an edit:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Selecting “inactivation” in the Loan Level Reporting tool or
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Reporting loan-level reporting exception code 40 (Inactivation)
elements.label: text

elements.text: To clear the edit, the Servicer must report $0 principal and $0 scheduled interest and not
elements.label: text

elements.text: advance the DDLPI until at least one full biweekly payment is collected from the Borrower.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Mortgages where the Borrower and Servicer agree to a biweekly payment plan,
elements.label: text

elements.text: pursuant to Section 8104.1(b), after the Mortgage was delivered to Freddie Mac must be
elements.label: text

elements.text: inactivated in accordance with this Section 8303.3(i).
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must not report the forecasted scheduled interest in the month for which the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage is inactivated.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Once the Servicer has inactivated the Mortgage, the Servicer must report it to Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: monthly as principal and interest activity with zero principal and zero interest due. The
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer must continue to report it in this manner until the Mortgage is reactivated by a full
elements.label: text

elements.text: or partial reinstatement, paid off, sold at foreclosure sale or transferred to REO. The Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: may accept and report partial payments, in increments of one full monthly payment for
elements.label: text

elements.text: Inactive Mortgages.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: See Section 8303.3(j)(ii) for information on partial reinstatements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (j) Reinstatement
elements.label: text

elements.text: Reinstatement is the process of restoring a delinquent Mortgage or a Mortgage in foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: to current status. A full reinstatement requires full payment of all past-due amounts and
elements.label: text

elements.text: brings an Inactive Mortgage current. A partial reinstatement occurs when the Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: makes a payment (at minimum, at least one full monthly principal payment and delinquent
elements.label: text

elements.text: interest, if applicable) on an Inactive Mortgage, but does not bring the Mortgage current. The
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer must comply with the requirements as specified in Sections 9203.1(c) through
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9203.1(g) regarding full and partial reinstatements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Full reinstatement of an Inactive Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must complete the following steps to report and remit when the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: fully reinstates an Inactive Mortgage:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Report all delinquent principal and interest collected from the Borrower and
elements.label: text

elements.text: forecasted scheduled interest for the next Accounting Cycle as follows:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If the DDLPI is on or before the inactivation date, Servicers must report principal
elements.label: text

elements.text: from the previously reported DDLPI to the current Accounting Cycle (or later)
elements.label: text

elements.text: and interest from the inactivation date to the current Accounting Cycle plus
elements.label: text

elements.text: forecasted scheduled interest for the next Accounting Cycle
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If the DDLPI is after the inactivation date, Servicers must report principal from
elements.label: text

elements.text: the previously reported DDLPI to the current Accounting Cycle (or later) and
elements.label: text

elements.text: interest from the previously reported DDLPI to the current Accounting Cycle plus
elements.label: text

elements.text: forecasted scheduled interest for the next Accounting Cycle
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If the Servicer reports principal and interest on or prior to the P&I Determination
elements.label: text

elements.text: Date, Freddie Mac will draft the full amount of any principal due to Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: on the P&I Draft Date of the current Accounting Cycle. If reported after the P&I
elements.label: text

elements.text: Determination Date, Freddie Mac will draft any principal due to Freddie Mac on
elements.label: text

elements.text: the P&I Draft Date of the following Accounting Cycle.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Delinquent and forecasted scheduled interest will be drafted on the P&I Draft
elements.label: text

elements.text: Date of the following Accounting Cycle
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Resume monthly P&I reporting on this Mortgage in future Accounting Cycles unless
elements.label: text

elements.text: the reinstatement resulted in a payoff.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Servicers are encouraged, but not required, to report Loan Level Reporting
elements.label: text

elements.text: exception code 50 (Reinstatement). If exception code 50 is not entered prior to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: end of the Accounting Cycle, Freddie Mac will update the Loan Level Reporting
elements.label: text

elements.text: system with a reinstatement transaction.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Partial reinstatement of an Inactive Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must complete the following steps to report when the Servicer partially
elements.label: text

elements.text: reinstates an Inactive Mortgage:
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer accepts at least one full monthly principal payment and delinquent
elements.label: text

elements.text: interest, then the Servicer must advance the DDLPI one month for each full payment
elements.label: text

elements.text: received. Until brought current, the Inactivation Date will not change, and the Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: will remain inactive regardless of the number of delinquent payments accepted by the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the DDLPI is on or prior to the Inactivation Date, the Servicer must report the principal
elements.label: text

elements.text: (increments of at least one full expected principal payment as of the new DDLPI)
elements.label: text

elements.text: accepted and zero interest. If the Servicer reports the partial reinstatement on or before
elements.label: text

elements.text: the P&I Determination Date, Freddie Mac will draft the principal on the P&I Draft Date.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If reported after the P&I Determination Date, Freddie Mac will draft the principal on the
elements.label: text

elements.text: P&I Draft Date in the following Accounting Cycle.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer advances the DDLPI beyond the Inactivation Date, the Servicer must
elements.label: text

elements.text: report the principal (increments of at least one full expected principal payment as of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: new DDLPI) received and the Freddie Mac expected delinquent interest.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer reports the partial reinstatement on or before the P&I Determination Date,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will draft the principal on the P&I Draft Date. If reported after the P&I
elements.label: text

elements.text: Determination Date, Freddie Mac will draft the principal on the P&I Draft Date in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: following Accounting Cycle. Delinquent interest will be drafted on the P&I Draft Date in
elements.label: text

elements.text: the following Accounting Cycle.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (k) Principal balance correction
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer reports an erroneous principal reduction amount, the Servicer must comply
elements.label: text

elements.text: with the following requirements:
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Understated principal reduction
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer understates a principal reduction during an Accounting Cycle, it may
elements.label: text

elements.text: adjust the amount of the understatement during the current Accounting Cycle’s loan￾level transaction by increasing the amount of total principal reduction. However, if the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer is unable to correct the understated amount during the current Accounting
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cycle, then they must adjust the amount of the understatement in the following
elements.label: text

elements.text: Accounting Cycle.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Overstated principal reduction
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer overstates a principal reduction during an Accounting Cycle, then it may
elements.label: text

elements.text: adjust the amount of the overstatement during the current Accounting Cycle's loan-level
elements.label: text

elements.text: transaction by reporting Loan Level Reporting exception code 80 (Principal Balance
elements.label: text

elements.text: Correction) and decreasing the amount of total principal reduction.
elements.label: text

elements.text: However, if the Servicer is unable to correct the overstated amount during the current
elements.label: text

elements.text: Accounting Cycle, then they must adjust the amount of the understatement in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: following Accounting Cycle. This will result in an increase in the UPB of that Mortgage,
elements.label: text

elements.text: which Freddie Mac refers to as negative principal reduction.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer may confirm the processing of the UPB adjustment on the Negative
elements.label: text

elements.text: Principal Reduction Report and the Loan Reconciliation Difference Report accessible
elements.label: text

elements.text: through Freddie Mac Gateway®. Freddie Mac may contact the Servicer should it require
elements.label: text

elements.text: additional documentation or information regarding the negative principal reduction.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (l) Deed-in-lieu of foreclosure reporting and remittance requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: A deed-in-lieu of foreclosure is a Borrower’s voluntary conveyance of clear title to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: property in exchange for a discharge of debt. The Servicer must comply with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements of Sections 9209.1 through 9209.6(b) regarding a deed-in-lieu of foreclosure.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must submit two separate transactions to Freddie Mac to report a deed-in-lieu of
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure as follows:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Report the deed execution in Resolve (see Exhibit 88) within one Business Day of
elements.label: text

elements.text: receiving the executed deed or, in the case of a leasehold Mortgage, an executed lease
elements.label: text

elements.text: assignment or new lease (collectively the “lease”). If the Borrower was required to make
elements.label: text

elements.text: a cash contribution as a condition of acceptance of the deed-in-lieu of foreclosure, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer must remit the funds, see Section 9209.6 for remittance requirements, no later
elements.label: text

elements.text: than the fifth Business Day after the receipt of funds.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. In the Loan Level Reporting tool (see Exhibit 88), report the Mortgage as a Transfer to
elements.label: text

elements.text: REO transaction by the end of the Accounting Cycle in which the Servicer receives the
elements.label: text

elements.text: executed deed or executed lease
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Mortgage was inactive as of the previous Accounting Cycle, a credit for the
elements.label: text

elements.text: reimbursement of advanced interest (calculated from the DDLPI up to, but not including, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: month the Servicer inactivated the Mortgage), if applicable, will be reflected in the current
elements.label: text

elements.text: Accounting Cycle Draft Report if reported as REO on or before the P&I Determination Date
elements.label: text

elements.text: or the next Accounting Cycle if reported as REO after the P&I Determination Date.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Mortgage was active as of the previous Accounting Cycle, a credit for the
elements.label: text

elements.text: reimbursement of advanced interest (calculated from the DDLPI through the end of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: previous Accounting Cycle), if applicable, will be reflected in the current Accounting Cycle
elements.label: text

elements.text: Draft Report if reported as REO on or before the P&I Determination Date or the next
elements.label: text

elements.text: Accounting Cycle if reported as REO after the P&I Determination Date.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Do not report on this Mortgage for future Accounting Cycles.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (m) FHA and VA foreclosure conveyance reporting and drafting requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: An FHA and VA foreclosure conveyance occurs when the Servicer forecloses on a Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: insured by the FHA or guaranteed by the VA and the property is not sold to a third party at
elements.label: text

elements.text: the foreclosure sale. The Servicer must comply with the requirements of Chapter 9301 and
elements.label: text

elements.text: Sections 9603.2(a) and 9603.2(b) regarding FHA and VA foreclosures.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In accordance with Section 9301.9(a), the Servicer must notify Freddie Mac of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure sale no later than one Business Day immediately following the date of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure sale.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Mortgage was inactive as of the previous Accounting Cycle, a credit for the
elements.label: text

elements.text: reimbursement of advanced interest (calculated from the DDLPI up to, but not including, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: month the Servicer inactivated the Mortgage), if applicable, will be reflected in the current
elements.label: text

elements.text: Accounting Cycle Draft Report if reported as FHA and VA conveyance on or before the P&I
elements.label: text

elements.text: Determination Date or the next Accounting Cycle if reported as FHA and VA conveyance
elements.label: text

elements.text: after the P&I Determination Date.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Mortgage was active as of the previous Accounting Cycle, a credit for the
elements.label: text

elements.text: reimbursement of advanced interest (calculated from the DDLPI through the end of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: previous Accounting Cycle), if applicable, will be reflected in the current Accounting Cycle
elements.label: text

elements.text: Draft Report if reported as FHA and VA conveyance on or before the P&I Determination
elements.label: text

elements.text: Date or the next Accounting Cycle if reported as FHA and VA conveyance after the P&I
elements.label: text

elements.text: Determination Date.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must not report on this Mortgage for future Accounting Cycles.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (n) REO reporting and drafting requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: A transfer to REO occurs when the Mortgaged Premises is not sold to a third party at the
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure sale and Freddie Mac acquires the property. The Servicer must comply with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements of Sections 8106.2(b), 9301.9(a) and 9301.9(b) and Chapter 9603 regarding the
elements.label: text

elements.text: submission on Freddie Mac’s behalf of IRS Form 1099-A, Acquisition or Abandonment of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Secured Property.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In accordance with Section 9301.9(a), the Servicer must notify Freddie Mac of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure sale no later than one Business Day immediately following the date of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure sale.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must complete the following steps to report for a transfer to REO:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. The Servicer must report the transfer of the property to REO in the Loan Level Reporting
elements.label: text

elements.text: tool (see Exhibit 88) in the same Accounting Cycle in which the foreclosure sale occurs
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. For the interest the Servicer advanced for the delinquent Mortgage, the Servicer will be
elements.label: text

elements.text: provided a credit calculated as follows:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If the Mortgage was inactive as of the end of the Accounting Cycle prior to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure, a credit for the reimbursement of advanced interest, if applicable, will be
elements.label: text

elements.text: reflected in the current Accounting Cycle Draft Report if reported as REO on or
elements.label: text

elements.text: before the P&I Determination Date, or the next Accounting Cycle if reported as REO
elements.label: text

elements.text: after the P&I Determination Date
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If the Mortgage was active as of the end of the Accounting Cycle prior to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure, a credit for the reimbursement of advanced interest, if applicable, will be
elements.label: text

elements.text: reflected in the current Accounting Cycle Draft Report if reported as REO on or
elements.label: text

elements.text: before the P&I Determination Date, or the next Accounting Cycle if reported as REO
elements.label: text

elements.text: after the P&I Determination Date
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. Do not report on this Mortgage in future Accounting Cycles
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers are not required to wait until the end of the redemption period to report the REO
elements.label: text

elements.text: transaction to recover the delinquent interest advanced to Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 8303.4: Error management and reconciliation in
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: reporting (08/13/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Correcting reporting errors
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Monthly portfolio reconciliation
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Freddie Mac’s reports to Servicers of discrepancies
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Daily edit reports
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Monthly reports provided to Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Reports to reconcile monthly activity
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Correcting reporting errors
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must monitor all Edit Error reports (Edits to be Cleared, System Cleared Edits,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Warning and Loan Level Missing) provided by Freddie Mac daily. If the Servicer discovers
elements.label: text

elements.text: an error after it has transmitted its accounting reporting information, the Servicer must take
elements.label: text

elements.text: immediate action to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Correct the source of the error
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Transmit a revision to Freddie Mac that reflects the Borrower’s payment activity or
elements.label: text

elements.text: disposition of the Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: If unable to resolve the issue, the Servicer should call Freddie Mac (see Directory 7) for
elements.label: text

elements.text: instructions on how to correct the information. Servicers may refer to the Investor
elements.label: text

elements.text: Reporting Training webpage for a detailed description of edit codes.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Edit Error reports may be accessed through the Loan Level Reporting tool (see Exhibit
elements.label: text

elements.text: 88, Servicing Tools).
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer fails to send Freddie Mac the appropriate information to clear any reporting
elements.label: text

elements.text: errors within the applicable time frame, Freddie Mac will make those corrections on the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer’s behalf subject to the applicable fees.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer reports a payoff to Freddie Mac in error, the Servicer must transmit a revision
elements.label: text

elements.text: within the same Accounting Cycle in which the Servicer erroneously reported it. If the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer reports a payoff to Freddie Mac in error and the Servicer does not reverse it within
elements.label: text

elements.text: the same Accounting Cycle, Freddie Mac will not be able to reactivate the Mortgage for the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer. The Servicer must advance its own funds which Freddie Mac will draft to liquidate
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Mortgage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must maintain accurate records for Freddie Mac’s review and inspection of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: actual principal reductions and adjustments made to correct any reporting errors.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Monthly portfolio reconciliation
elements.label: text

elements.text: Within 30 days after the end of each Accounting Cycle, the Servicer must reconcile each data
elements.label: text

elements.text: element included in the monthly trial balance report (i.e., interest bearing UPB, non-interest
elements.label: text

elements.text: bearing UPB, DDLPI, principal and interest constant, Note Rate and Accounting Net Yield
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ANY)) to its internal records.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers must prepare a separate monthly reconciliation for each unique Seller/Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: number on either the online version of Form 75, Reconciliation of Mortgage Portfolio, or a
elements.label: text

elements.text: copy that is an exact replica of the form.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Upon Freddie Mac’s request, the Servicer must be able to provide Freddie Mac with a copy
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the completed Form 75, together with any supporting documentation, for up to one year
elements.label: text

elements.text: from the date of the related reconciliation worksheet. All documentation must be received by
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac no later than the requested due date. Refer to the Form 75 Reconciliation for
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage Portfolio Tutorial available on the Investor Accounting Training webpage for
elements.label: text

elements.text: additional information to complete Form 75.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Freddie Mac’s reports to Servicers of discrepancies
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will process the data the Servicer transmits, compare the data daily against
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac’s database and notify the Servicer of any discrepancies on the Edit Reports
elements.label: text

elements.text: daily. The Servicer is responsible for ensuring the accuracy of the data the Servicer submits
elements.label: text

elements.text: to Freddie Mac. If applicable, any discrepancies identified by Freddie Mac prior to the P&I
elements.label: text

elements.text: Determination Date must be resolved on or before the P&I Determination Date.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Discrepancies identified after the P&I Determination Date must be resolved prior to the end
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the current Accounting Cycle.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Refer to Sections 8303.4(a) and 8303.4(c) for additional information.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Daily edit reports
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must monitor the daily edit reports provided by Freddie Mac through the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac Loan Level Reporting tool available via Freddie Mac Gateway® (see Exhibit 88)
elements.label: text

elements.text: and promptly correct the errors indicated on the reports (refer to Section 8303.4(b)). Failure
elements.label: text

elements.text: to promptly correct the errors indicated on the reports may negatively impact the Servicer’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer Success Scorecard.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: See Section 3501.2 for additional information about the Servicer Success Scorecard.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The daily edit reports provided by Freddie Mac are:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Freddie Mac Edits to be Cleared Report: This daily report identifies loan-level
elements.label: text

elements.text: transaction edits that prevent Freddie Mac from processing transactions the Servicer has
elements.label: text

elements.text: reported. The Servicer must take immediate action to resolve the edit errors and transmit
elements.label: text

elements.text: revised transactions to Freddie Mac that reflect the Borrower’s payment activity.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Freddie Mac System Cleared Edits Report: This daily report identifies loan-level
elements.label: text

elements.text: transaction edits that Freddie Mac’s system changed and continued processing. The
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer must review and determine the cause of the edit and take action, if necessary, by
elements.label: text

elements.text: the end of the current Accounting Cycle to prevent the edit from recurring in future
elements.label: text

elements.text: Accounting Cycles.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Warning Report: This daily report identifies loan-level transactions that were accepted
elements.label: text

elements.text: by Freddie Mac that don’t require the Servicer to take action but for which Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: recommends a review. Servicers must use this report to determine whether the correct
elements.label: text

elements.text: loan transaction was processed. If the correct loan transaction was not processed, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer must submit a revision.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Loan Level Missing Report: This report identifies Mortgages missing from the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer’s monthly loan-level transactions. This includes any Mortgage:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ That the Servicer did not report to Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ With unresolved edits on the Edits to be Cleared Report
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Reported to Freddie Mac with an invalid Freddie Mac loan number, and/or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ For which Freddie Mac received a transaction but the Mortgage was rejected by
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac’s system
elements.label: text
